1.ja: Fushigidane may be a combination of 不思議 fushigi (strange; mysterious) and 種 tane (seed), as well as a pun of the phrase 不思議だね Fushigi da ne? ("Isn't it strange?").
1.en: Bulbasaur is a combination of bulb (a rounded underground storage organ present in some plants) and dinosaur or -saurus (common suffix used in the names of extinct reptilian creatures, derived from the Ancient Greek word for lizard). The name also sounds similar to the word bulbous.

2.ja: Fushigisou may be a combination of 不思議 fushigi (strange; mysterious) and 草 sō (grass), as well as a pun of the phrase 不思議そう Fushigi sō ("It seems strange").
2.en: Ivysaur may be a combination of ivy (woody climbing or trailing plant genus) and dinosaur, and may also incorporate poison ivy (referring to its Grass/Poison-type designation).

3.ja: Fushigibana may be a combination of 不思議 fushigi (strange; mysterious) and 花 hana (flower).
3.en: Venusaur may be a combination of Venus (referring to the Venus flytrap plant) or venom and dinosaur.

4.ja: Hitokage may be a combination of 火 hi (fire) and 蜥蜴 tokage (lizard), forming together the word 火蜥蜴 hitokage (mythical salamander).
4.en: Charmander is a combination of char (to burn) and salamander, as stated in the January 1999 "POCKEMON JOURNAL" on the official Game Freak website. Alternatively, in a 2016 interview with Tsunekazu Ishihara by INSIDE, Charmander is stated to be a combination of charcoal (carbon residue produced by strongly heating wood) and Salamandra (a genus of salamander including four species of fire salamander

5.ja: Lizardo may be derived from the transcription of the English word lizard.
5.en: Charmeleon may be a combination of char (to burn) and chameleon.

6.ja: Lizardon may be a combination of lizard and either dragon or -don (common suffix in dinosaur names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).
6.en: Charizard may be a combination of char (to burn) and lizard.

7.ja: Zenigame may be a combination of 銭 zeni (money) and 亀 kame (turtle), forming the word 銭亀 zenigame (baby Japanese pond turtle). According to 's television program , "'Zeni' is an old-fashioned word for money. Squirtle's shells look like old coins, so that's how they got their name!"
7.en: Squirtle is a combination of squirt (to spray) and turtle. The name was chosen in apparent reference to water guns, which were thought of as "cool" toys for children. "Squirt" is also a slang term for a small child.

8.ja: Kameil may be a combination of 亀 kame (turtle, tortoise) and tail.
8.en: Wartortle may be a combination of war or warrior, water, tortoise, and turtle.

9.ja: Kamex may be a combination of 亀 kame (tortoise, turtle) and マックス makkusu (max; maximum).
9.en: Blastoise may be a combination of blast (referring to its cannons) and tortoise.

10.ja: Caterpie may be a corruption or shortening of caterpillar. It may also be an attempt to transcribe the Japanese name,「キャタピー」(Caterpie), into English.

11.ja: Transel may be a combination of transform and or possibly a pun on ランドセル randoseru (school bag).
11.en: Metapod may be a combination of metamorphosis and pod (a shell containing seeds).

12.ja: Butterfree may be a combination of butterfly and free. It may also involve パタパタ patapata (the sound of wings flapping) and 振る furu (to flap).

13.ja: Beedle may be a combination of bee and needle.
13.en: Weedle may be a combination of worm and needle.

14.en: Kakuna may be a corruption of .

15.ja: Spear is literally spear. It may also be derived from アピス Apis (the genus name of the honey bee) spelled backwards.
15.en: Beedrill may be a combination of bee and drill.

16.ja: Poppo may be derived from ぽっぽ poppo (onomatopoetic sound of a pigeon's coo).
16.en: Pidgey may be a corruption of pigeon or a combination of , chickadee, and pudgy (small and plump).

17.ja: Pidgeotto may be a combination of and the Italian diminutive suffix -otto. It is also very similar to the romanized spelling of Pidgeot's Japanese name, ピジョット pijotto.

18.ja: pigeon (英語:ハト)、parrot (英語:オウム)、jet (英語:ジェット)
18.en: Pidgeot and Pigeot may be a combination of and (referring to its speedy flight).

19.ja: Koratta may be a combination of 子 ko (child) or 小 ko (small) and rat.
19.en: Rattata may be a combination of rat and attack, as indicated by its pre-release name. It may also incorporate rat-a-tat (a tapping sound).

20.ja: Ratta may be derived from ラット ratto (rat).
20.en: Raticate may be a combination of rat and eradicate (to destroy or exterminate) or masticate (to chew).

21.ja: Onisuzume may be a combination of 鬼 oni (ogre) and 雀 suzume (sparrow).
21.en: Spearow may be a combination of spear (referring to its sharp beak) and .

22.ja: Onidrill may be a combination of 鬼 oni (demon), 鳥 tori (bird), and ドリル doriru (drill).
22.en: Fearow may be a combination of fear (referring to its intimidating appearance) and . It may also involve feather and arrow (referring to its sharp beak).

23.ja: The Japanese name Arbo (アーボ) may be derived from cobra, or (ボア) spelled backwards.

24.ja: cobra（英語: コブラ）の逆さ読み
24.en: Arbok is cobra spelled backwards, but with the c replaced with a k.

25.ja: Pikachu is a combination of ピカピカ pikapika (onomatopoeia for sparkle) and チューチュー chūchū (the sound of a mouse squeaking).

26.ja: Raichu may be a combination of 雷 rai (thunder) and チューチュー chūchū (onomatopoeia for the squeak of a mouse).

27.en: Sandshrew may be a combination of sand and shrew.

28.ja: Sandpan may be a combination of sand and pangolin.
28.en: Sandslash may be a combination of sand and slash.

29.ja: Nidoran♀ may be derived from needle, with ♀ as an indication of its female gender. It may also involve 二度 nido (twice).

30.ja: Nidorina may be a combination of needle, rhinoceros, and -ina (feminine diminutive suffix in Spanish and Italian). It may also involve 二度 nido (twice).

31.ja: Nidoqueen may be a combination of needle and queen. It may also involve 二度 nido (twice).

32.ja: Nidoran♂ may be derived from needle, with ♂ as an indication of its male gender. It may also involve 二度 nido (twice).

33.ja: Nidorino may be a combination of needle, rhinoceros, and -ino (masculine diminutive suffix in Spanish and Italian). It may also involve 二度 nido (twice).

34.ja: Nidoking may be a combination of needle and king. It may also involve 二度 nido (twice).

35.ja: Pippi may be derived from the repetition of the first syllable of pixie.
35.en: Clefairy may be a combination of clef (musical symbol) and fairy.

36.ja: Pixy may be derived from pixie.
36.en: Clefable may be a combination of clef (musical symbol) and fable.

37.ja: Its Alolan name, Keokeo, may be derived from keʻokeʻo (Hawaiian for white).
37.en: Vulpix may be a combination of vulpes (Latin for fox) and six. It may also involve , the Roman god of fire.

38.ja: Kyukon may be a combination of 九 kyū (nine) or 九尾狐 kyūbiko () and こん kon (onomatopoetic sound of a fox's cry). It may also involve 旧恨 kyūkon (old grudge) and 焜炉 konro (gas stove).
38.en: Ninetales may be a combination of nine and tales, referring to fairy tales (alluding to it being based on a mythical creature) as well as being a homophone to tails. It may also reference the cat o' nine tails whip (due to its tendency to punish anyone who grabs its tails).

39.ja: Purin may refer to プリン (pudding; referring to its soft body). It may also involve プチ puchi (petite) and pretty.
39.en: Jigglypuff may be a combination of jiggly and puff (to swell in size; a burst of air).

40.ja: Pukurin may be a combination of ぷくぷく pukupuku (cute and chubby), ぷりぷり puripuri (angrily or in a huff; referring to its personality), くりくり kurikuri (big and round), and プリン (pudding; referring to its soft body). It may also involve 風船 fūsen (balloon) and 膨れる fukureru (to become inflated).
40.en: Wigglytuff may be a combination of wiggly and tuft (a bunch of hair) or tough.

41.ja: Zubat may be a combination of bat and ずばっと zubatto (piercingly). Zubat also shares a name with the 1977 tokusatsu superhero series Kaiketsu Zubat.

42.ja: ghoul (英語: グルー)、gore (英語: 流血)、bat (英語: コウモリ)
42.en: Golbat may be a combination of gore, ghoul, or gulp and bat.

43.ja: Nazonokusa may be a play on 謎の草 nazo no kusa (enigmatic herb).
43.en: Oddish may be derived from oddish (somewhat odd) or a combination of odd (strange) and radish.

44.ja: Kusaihana may be derived from 臭い花 kusai hana (stinking flower).
44.en: Gloom may be derived from gloom (a state of depression), glue (due to the sticky substance), and . It may also involve glume (a leaf-like structure).

45.ja: Ruffresia may be derived from Rafflesia.
45.en: Vileplume may be a combination of vile (causing physical or mental repulsion) and plume (object that resembles a feather; cloud of dispersed substance) or .

46.en: Paras may be derived from , referring to the parasitic mushroom growing on its back.

47.ja: parasite (英語: 寄生虫)、insect (英語: 昆虫)
47.en: Parasect may be a combination of and insect.

48.ja: Kongpang may be derived from compound eye (visual organ found in insects) or 昆虫 konchū (insect).
48.en: Venonat may be combination of venom and gnat (a small insect).

49.ja: Morphon may be derived from metamorphosis or the butterfly genus Morpho.
49.en: Venomoth may be a combination of venom and moth.

50.ja: Digda may be derived from dig.
50.en: Diglett may be a combination of dig and the suffix -let (indicating small size).

51.ja: dug (英語: 掘った)、trio (イタリア語: 三人組)
51.en: Dugtrio may be a combination of dug and trio (a group of three).

52.ja: Nyarth may be a play on ニャー nyā (the Japanese onomatopoeia for the sound a cat makes).
52.en: Meowth may be a combination of meow (the sound a cat makes), mouth, and wealth, alluding to the Maneki-neko's capacity to bring good fortune. The -th may also be borrowed from Nyarth, as both meow and nyā are onomatopoeia of a cat's cry.

53.ja: Persian (英語: ペルシャネコ)
53.en: Persian may refer to Persian cats or possibly Persian leopards. It could also be related to purr, the onomatopoeia for the noise a cat makes when content.

54.ja: Koduck may be a combination of 小 ko (small) or 子 ko (child) and duck.
54.en: Psyduck may be a combination of psy- or psychic (referring to its mental powers) and duck.

55.en: Golduck may be a combination of gold and duck. While Golduck itself is not colored gold, gold is often used to e.g., the is the Gummi variety loved by Psychic-type Pokémon, and the Marsh's Japanese name is "Gold Badge".

56.ja: monkey（英語:サル）、mandrill（英語:マンドリル）、man（英語:人間）、mad（英語:頭に来ている）、ヤンキー
56.en: Mankey may be a combination of man and monkey. It may also involve mad and cranky.

57.ja: Okorizaru may be derived from 怒り猿 okorizaru (angry monkey).
57.en: Primeape may be a combination of prime, primate, and ape.

58.ja: Gardie may refer to its abilities as a , as a corruption of guard or guardian, with the suffix ‑ie (forming nouns or names, often diminutive, affectionate or colloquial) applied.
58.en: Growlithe may be a combination of growl and lithe (flexible).

59.ja: Windie may refer to wind or windy, reflecting its defining nature of being fast like the wind, further emphasized by Extreme Speed being its signature move in Generation II.
59.en: Arcanine may be a combination of arcane and canine.

60.ja: Nyoromo may be a combination of ニョロニョロ nyoronyoro (onomatopoeia for wriggling) and 子供 kodomo (child).
60.en: Poliwag may be derived from polliwog (outdated term for tadpole), which is formed from Middle English words pol (head) and wiglen (to wiggle). It may also involve wag (to swing from side to side).

61.ja: Nyorozo may be a combination of ニョロニョロ nyoronyoro (onomatopoeia for wriggling) and 小僧 kozō (youngster).
61.en: Poliwhirl is a combination of (outdated term for tadpole) and whirl.

62.ja: Nyorobon may be a combination of ニョロニョロ nyoronyoro (onomatopoeia for wriggling) and 坊 bon (boy).
62.en: Poliwrath may be a combination of (outdated term for tadpole) and wrath.

63.ja: Casey may be named after Edgar Cayce, a purported psychic famous for entering self-induced trances.
63.en: Abra may be derived from abracadabra, an incantation used in .

64.ja: Yungerer may be named after Uri Geller, an illusionist famed for his spoon-bending technique. This contributed to Geller Nintendo for using his likeness in a character.
64.en: Kadabra may be derived from abracadabra, an incantation used in .

65.ja: Foodin may be named after Harry Houdini, a famed illusionist and escape artist.
65.en: Alakazam's name is derived from alakazam, an incantation used in similar to abracadabra.

66.ja: Wanriky is derived from 腕 wan (arm) and 力 riki (strength); altogether, it is derived from an alternate reading of 腕力 wanryoku (arm strength, physical strength).
66.en: Machop is a combination of macho (manly; Spanish and Portuguese for male) and chop.

67.ja: Goriky may be derived from 剛力 gōriki (herculean strength).
67.en: Machoke may be a combination of macho (manly; Spanish and Portuguese for male) and choke.

68.ja: Kairiky may be derived from 怪力 kairiki (superhuman strength), which is the name of its category in Japanese.
68.en: Machamp may be a combination of macho (manly; Spanish and Portuguese for male) and champ.

69.ja: Madatsubomi may be derived from まだ蕾 mada tsubomi (still in bud).
69.en: Bellsprout may be a combination of bell and sprout.

70.ja: Utsudon may be derived from 靫葛 utsubokazura (pitcher plant). It may also involve 鬱 utsu (depression, melancholy).
70.en: Weepinbell may be a combination of weeping and bell.

71.ja: Utsubot may be derived from 靫葛 utsubokazura (pitcher plant) and possibly ポット pot.
71.en: Victreebel may be a combination of victory, tree, and bell. The removal of an L in "bell" is likely a result of the ten-character limit on English Pokémon names prior to Generation VI.

72.ja: Menokurage may be a combination of 目の敵 me no kataki (enemy) or 目の毒 me no doku (temptation, literally "poison to the eye") and 水母 kurage (jellyfish).
72.en: Tentacool may be a combination of tentacle and cool. It may also involve Tentaculata (class of comb jellies).

73.ja: Dokukurage may be a combination of 毒 doku (poison) and 水母 kurage (jellyfish).
73.en: Tentacruel may be a combination of tentacle and cruel.

74.ja: Isitsubute is a combination of 石 ishi (stone; rock) and 礫 tsubute (pebble, stone, or a small throwing stone).
74.en: Geodude is a combination of geo- (prefix for earth) and dude (slang word for a male person). It may also involve geode (hollow rock with crystals lined inside) and rude.

75.ja: Golone may be a combination of ゴロンゴロン goron-goron (onomatopoeia of rolling rocks) and stone.
75.en: Graveler may be a combination of gravel and traveler.

76.ja: Golonya may be a combination of ゴロンゴロン goron-goron (onomatopoeia of rolling rocks) and montagna (Italian for mountain).

77.ja: pony（英語：ポニー、子馬）、bonita（スペイン語：可愛い）
77.en: Ponyta may be a combination of pony and bonita (pretty in various Iberian languages).

78.ja: Gallop is literally gallop, a form of running for hooved animals.
78.en: Rapidash may be a combination of rapid and dash (referring to the high speed at which it runs) or ash (referencing its Fire type).

79.ja: Yadon may be a combination of 宿借り yadokari (), 宿主 yadonushi (parasitic host), and 鈍 don (dull). It may also be a shortening of ヤドラン Yadoran, Slowbro's Japanese name.
79.en: Slowpoke is literally slowpoke, an American informal word for someone who is slow.

80.ja: Yadoran may involve 宿借り yadokari (hermit crab), 宿主 yadonushi (parasitic host), and 鈍 don (dull). According to singer-songwriter Jadranka Stojaković, Yadoran was named after her first name (transliterated as ヤドランカ Yadoranka in Japanese). Stojaković had worked for Konami during her residence in Japan, and a former colleague of hers who later joined Game Freak brought her name to mind while
80.en: Slowbro may be a combination of slow and bro (short for brother).

81.ja: Coil may refer to electromagnetic coil.
81.en: Magnemite may be a combination of magnet and mite (minute object). It may also play on magnetite and/or maghemite, iron oxide minerals that can, under specific conditions, become permanent magnets.

82.ja: Rarecoil may be a combination of rare-earth magnet and electromagnetic coil.
82.en: Magneton may be a combination of magnet and ton. It may also be derived from magneton (various units of magnetic moment) or possibly (high-power vacuum tube).

83.ja: 鴨葱 (鴨が葱を背負って来る)

84.en: Doduo may be a combination of dodo (an extinct, flightless bird) and duo (a pair).

85.ja: Dodorio may be a combination of ドードー dodo, 鳥 tori (bird), and トリオ trio.
85.en: Dodrio may be a combination of dodo (an extinct, flightless bird) and trio (group of three).

86.ja: Pawou may be derived from an onomatopoeia of a seal's bark.

87.ja: Jugon may be derived from 儒艮 jugon (dugong).
87.en: Dewgong may be a combination of dew and dugong.

88.ja: Betbeter may be derived from べとべと betobeto and べたべた betabeta, both meaning sticky.
88.en: Grimer may be derived from grime (dirt or filth).

89.ja: Betbeton may be derived from べとべと betobeto (sticky) and ton.
89.en: Muk is a corruption of muck (generally something filthy).

90.en: Shellder may be derived from shell and possibly shelter.

91.ja: Parshen may be a combination of パール pearl and シェル .
91.en: Cloyster may be a combination of clam and oyster, as well as a play on cloister (to seclude or shelter oneself).

92.ja: Ghos may be derived from ghost and gas.
92.en: Gastly may be a combination of gas, ghost, and ghastly (frightful).

93.en: Haunter may be derived from haunt and the -er or possibly hunter.

94.ja: Gengar and Gangar may come from -ganger or -gänger (walker), as found in genganger or wiedergänger (types of revenants), or doppelgänger (ghostly double of a living person). It may also involve 幻 gen (phantasm).

95.ja: Iwark may be a combination of 岩 iwa (rock) and either ブルワーク bulwark or スネーク snake.
95.en: Onix may be derived from onyx (a mineral).

96.ja: Sleepe may be derived from sleep.
96.en: Drowzee may be a combination of drowsy and zzz (onomatopoeia for snoring).

97.en: Hypno may be derived from hypnosis.

98.en: Krabby may be a combination of crab and crabby (grouchy or ill-tempered).

99.ja: king (英語: 王)、crab (英語: カニ)、king crab (英語: タラバガニ)
99.en: Kingler may be a combination of king crab and angler. It may also involve fiddler crab.

100.ja: Biriridama may be a combination of ビリビリ biribiri (sound of electric shock) and 玉 tama (sphere).
100.en: Voltorb may be a combination of volt and orb.

101.ja: Marumine may be a combination of 丸 maru (circle) and mine, as in landmine.
101.en: Electrode may be a portmanteau of electric and explode or node (a computer or program), as well as a pun on electrode, a device through which electrical currents pass (although such a device is usually in the shape of a rod).

102.ja: Tamatama may be derived from 玉子 tamago (egg), as well as being a play on 偶々 tamatama (unexpectedly).
102.en: Exeggcute may be a combination of execute, egg, and cute.

103.ja: Nassy may be a combination of ココナッツ kokonattsu (coconuts) and 椰子 yashi (palm tree).
103.en: Exeggutor may be a combination of egg and executor.

104.ja: Karakara may be derived from カラカラ karakara (onomatopoeia for light clattering), contrasting with the ガラガラ garagara (onomatopoeia for heavy clattering) in Marowak's Japanese name.
104.en: Cubone is a combination of cub and bone.

105.ja: Garagara may be derived from ガラガラ garagara (onomatopoeia for heavy clattering), contrasting with the カラカラ karakara (onomatopoeia for light clattering) in Cubone's Japanese name.
105.en: Marowak may be a combination of bone marrow and whack.

106.ja: Sawamular may be derived from Tadashi Sawamura, a famous kickboxer.
106.en: Hitmonlee is named after Bruce Lee, and may additionally involve a combination of hit and monster. The name is also related to Hitmonchan and Hitmontop, both of which are parallel Evolutions to it as of Generation II.

107.ja: Ebiwalar may be derived from the Japanese world champion boxer Hiroyuki Ebihara.
107.en: Hitmonchan is named after Jackie Chan, and may additionally involve a combination of hit and monster. The name is also related to Tyrogue's other Evolutions, Hitmonlee and Hitmontop.

108.ja: Beroringa may be a combination of べろ bero (colloquial term for tongue), ベロリ berori (licking), ベロベロ berobero (onomatopoeia for licking), and lingua (Latin for tongue).
108.en: Lickitung is a combination of lick and tongue.

109.ja: Dogars may be derived from 毒ガス doku-gasu (poison gas).
109.en: Koffing may be a corruption of coughing.

110.ja: Matadogas may come from 又 mata (again) and 毒ガス doku-gasu (poison gas). It may also be derived from マスタードガス masutādogasu (mustard gas).
110.en: Weezing may be a corruption of wheezing (producing a whistling sound while breathing with difficulty).

111.ja: Sihorn may be a combination of 犀 sai (rhinoceros) and horn.
111.en: Rhyhorn may be a combination of rhinoceros, rhyolite (a type of rock, referring to its Rock type), and horn.

112.ja: Sidon may be a combination of 犀 sai (rhinoceros) and don (Spanish for lord) or -don (common suffix in extinct animal names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).
112.en: Rhydon may be a combination of rhinoceros, rhyolite (a type of rock, referring to its Rock type), and don (Spanish for lord) or -don (common suffix in extinct animal names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).

113.en: Chansey may be from either chance or chancy.

114.ja: Monjara may be a combination of monster, もじゃもじゃ mojamoja (shaggy or unkempt), and 毛むくじゃら kemukujara (densely covered in thick hair).
114.en: Tangela may be a combination of tangle and -ella (suffix used to form taxonomic names).

115.ja: Garura may be a combination of kangaroo and ruler.
115.en: Kangaskhan may be a combination of kangaroo and , possibly in reference to Genghis Khan.

116.ja: Tattu may be derived from 竜 tatsu (dragon) or 竜の落とし子 tatsu-no-otoshigo (seahorse, lit. "illegitimate child of a dragon").
116.en: Horsea may be derived from seahorse and horsie (childish term for a horse).

117.ja: sea (英語: 海) 、dragon (英語: ドラゴン)、seadragon (英語: シードラゴン)
117.en: Seadra may be a combination of sea, seahorse, dragon, (a type of animal related to seahorses), and (a mythological water creature).

118.ja: Tosakinto may be derived from 土佐金 tosakin (curly fantail goldfish), a distinctive breed of goldfish.
118.en: Goldeen may be a combination of goldfish or golden and queen.

119.ja: Azumao may be derived from 東錦 (a breed of goldfish) and 王 ō (king).
119.en: Seaking may be a combination of sea, king, and possibly a play on seeking.

120.ja: Hitodeman may be a combination of 海星 hitode (starfish) and man (possibly in reference to Ultraman).
120.en: Staryu may be a combination of star and you (in opposition to the me in Starmie).

121.ja: Starmie may be a combination of star and mystery. Its name in English may also involve me (in opposition to the you in Staryu). The name Starmie may also refer to ミミー Mimī, the early design of Staryu from the original Capsule Monsters concept.

122.ja: Barrierd may be a combination of barrier (referring to the invisible walls it can create) and weird.
122.en: Mr. Mime consists of Mr. (contraction of Mister) and mime.

123.en: Scyther may be a combination of scythe and the suffix -er (something that does an action).

124.ja: Rougela may be derived from , a type of makeup and also the French word for red, and also from the French verb geler (to freeze, to be freezing cold).
124.en: Jynx may be a corruption of jinx, a curse or hex of bad luck.

125.ja: Eleboo may be a combination of electric or エレキ eleki (electric) and ブンブン bunbun (buzz).
125.en: Electabuzz may be a combination of electric and buzz (possibly referring to the humming sound of electricity).

126.ja: Boober may be a combination of booby (another type of seabird) and a corruption of burn.
126.en: Magmar may be a combination of magma and fulmar (a seabird known for projectile-vomiting stomach oil as a defense mechanism).

127.ja: Kailios may be a combination of the Greek letter (カイ kai), 刈 kai (shear), 魁偉 kaii (brawny), cross (referring to its ), and κοριός korios ( for bug).
127.en: Pinsir is a corruption of pincer, referring to its horns.

128.ja: Kentauros may be derived from Κένταυρος Kéntauros (Greek for centaur).
128.en: Tauros may be derived from its Japanese name, Kentauros. It may also be derived from ταῦρος tauros (Greek for bull).

129.ja: Koiking may be a combination of 鯉 koi (carp) and king, which may come from 金魚 kingyo (goldfish).
129.en: Magikarp may be a combination of magic (referring to its drastic power increase upon evolving into Gyarados or to magical goldfishes) and carp.

130.ja: According to Ken Sugimori, Gyarados's name has no specific origin and was chosen merely for its "force". However, it may be variously derived from 虐殺 gyakusatsu (slaughter), 逆境 gyakkyō (adversity), or 逆転 gyakuten (reversal, turnaround); 荒らす arasu (to devastate); and 脅す odosu (to threaten).

131.ja: Lapras may be a transcription error from the Japanese name「ラプラス」(Laplace), arising from "l" and "r" sounding similar in Japanese. Alternatively, the name may be a reference to lapis lazuli.
131.en: Laplace may be a reference to Pierre-Simon Laplace, a mathematician who wrote several books on the mathematical properties of the sea and tides. Alternatively, Laplace may be derived from la place (French for "the seat"), referring to how passengers sit on its back like a seat to be ferried across bodies of water, or from the phrase à la place (French for "instead", but literally "to the place"),

132.ja: Metamon may be a combination of metamorphose (to undergo a physical transformation) or meta- (prefix meaning "encompassing" or "transcending", which may refer to Transform allowing Ditto to take on the shape of every other Pokémon) and monster.
132.en: Ditto is literally ditto (that which was stated before; copy of a document; imitation).

133.en: Eevee and Eievui are pronunciations of E-V, the first two letters in the word Evolution.

134.ja: Showers corresponds to the English noun shower (plural showers), plausibly referring to or the bathroom shower, as well as the verb shower (third-person singular simple present showers), meaning either to spray with water or to use a shower.
134.en: Vaporeon may be a combination of vapor and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eevee Evolutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name.

135.en: Jolteon may be a combination of jolt and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eevee Evolutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name.

136.ja: Booster is literally booster, possibly referring to the .
136.en: Flareon may be a combination of flare and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eeveelutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name.

137.ja: Porygon is derived from ポリゴン porigon (polygon).

138.ja: Omnite may be a combination of 鸚鵡貝 ōmugai (nautilus) and ammonite.
138.en: Omanyte may be derived from ammonite.

139.ja: Omstar may be a combination of 鸚鵡貝 ōmugai (nautilus) and star.
139.en: Omastar may be a combination of its Japanese name, a phonetic anagram of ammonite, and star, for its body shape and five-pointed shell.

140.ja: Kabuto may be derived from 兜 kabuto (helmet) and 兜蟹 kabutogani (horseshoe crab).

141.ja: Kabutops may be derived from 兜蝦 kabutoebi (Triops), 兜 kabuto (helmet), Triops, and ‑ops (common suffix in names of prehistoric animals derived from the Ancient Greek word for face).

142.ja: Ptera may be derived from pterosaur or Pteranodon.
142.en: Aerodactyl may be a combination of ἀέρος aéros ( for air) or aeroplane, and .

143.ja: Kabigon is derived from Kirby (Japanese: カービィ Kābī), the eponymous main character of Nintendo's . In an interview with Game Informer, Junichi Masuda stated that Kōji Nishino, the main inspiration for Snorlax, used to be called "Kirby" by Game Freak staff as a nod to his large appetite.
143.en: Snorlax is a combination of snore and lax or lackadaisical. It was reportedly derived from Esquilax, the name of a nonexistent medieval creature described in The Simpsons episode "Lisa's Wedding". Snorlax may also involve relax.

144.ja: Freezer is literally freezer (that which freezes).
144.en: Articuno is a combination of arctic and uno (Spanish for one, indicating that it is the first of the Kanto legendary birds).

145.en: Zapdos is a combination of zap and dos (Spanish for two, indicating that it is the second in the series of Kanto legendary birds).

146.en: Moltres is a combination of molten and tres (Spanish for three, indicating that it is the third in the trio of Kanto legendary birds). It may also derive from .

147.ja: Miniryu may be a combination of miniature and 竜 ryū (dragon).
147.en: Dratini may be a combination of dragon and teeny, tiny, or mini.

148.ja: Hakuryū may be derived from 白竜 hakuryū (white dragon).
148.en: Dragonair may be a combination of dragon and air, debonair, or lair (traditional home of a dragon).

149.ja: Kairyu may be derived from 海竜 kairyū (sea dragon). It may also involve 怪獣 kaijū (monster), 快 kai (cheerful), and 破壊 hakai (destruction).
149.en: Dragonite may be a combination of dragon and knight, night, or draconite (a mythical stone said to be found in the heads of dragons and are pyramidal in shape, similar to Dragonite's horn).

150.ja: Mewtwo may be a combination of Mew or mutant (ミュータント) and two.

151.ja: Mew may be a combination of mutant or mutation and new. It may also involve 妙 myō (wonderful, exquisite).
151.en: According to Julien Bardakoff, the French localizer for Pokémon Red and Blue, he asked if Mew's name was based on the English onomatopoeia for a cat's meow and was explicitly told it was not.

152.ja: Happa is the Japanese word [https://jisho.org/word/葉っぱ 葉っぱ] happa, meaning "leaf".
152.en: Chikorita and Chicorita may be a combination of chicory (a flowering plant) and -ita (the feminine form of the Spanish suffix for something small or young).

153.ja: ベイリーフ (ローリエの別名)
153.en: Bayleef and Bayleaf derive from bay leaf, a herb used in cooking known for its pungent smell.

154.en: Meganium may be a combination of mega and geranium.

155.ja: Hinoarashi may be a combination of 火 hi (fire) and ヤマアラシ (山荒) yama-arashi (porcupine). It can be taken to mean 火の嵐 hi no arashi (storm of fire).
155.en: Cyndaquil may be a combination of cinder (ashes) and quill (referring to needle-like hairs).

156.ja: Magmarashi may be a combination of magma, 穴熊 anaguma (badger) and 山荒 yama-arashi (porcupine).
156.en: Quilava may be a combination of quill (a needle-like hair) and lava (extrusive molten rock).

157.ja: Bakphoon may be a combination of 爆風 bakufū (blast from an explosion) and typhoon.
157.en: Typhlosion may be a combination of typhoon and explosion. It could also derive from Typhon, a monster in Greek mythology who was said to be buried under the volcano Mount Etna after his defeat and who also had a mate named ; or Eulipotyphla, a mammalian order containing shrews, hedgehogs, moles, and solenodons.

158.ja: Waninoko may be taken to mean 鰐の子 wani no ko (child of a crocodile/alligator).
158.en: Totodile may be a combination of tot and crocodile.

159.ja: Alligates may be derived from alligator.
159.en: Croconaw may be a combination of crocodile and gnaw, though it may also involve maw or jaw.

160.ja: Ordile may be a combination of 王 ō (king), 皇 ō (emperor), or 大 ō (large), and crocodile.
160.en: Feraligatr may be a combination of feral or ferocious and alligator. The shortening of "gator" into gatr may be a result of the ten-character limit on English Pokémon names in Generation II.

161.ja: Otachi may be a combination of 尾 o (tail) and 立ち tachi (stand) or 鼬 itachi (weasel).
161.en: Sentret may be a combination of sentry and ferret.

162.ja: Ootachi may be a combination of 大 ō (big), 尾 o (tail), 立ち tachi (stand), and 鼬 itachi (weasel).
162.en: Furret may a combination of fur and ferret.

163.ja: Hoho is derived from ホー hō (the sound an owl makes).
163.en: Hoothoot is derived from hoot (the sound an owl makes). It may also involve cuckoo, as in a cuckoo clock.

164.ja: Yorunozuku may be a combination of 夜 yoru (night) and 木菟 mimizuku (horned owl).
164.en: Noctowl may be a combination of nocturnal and owl.

165.ja: ladybug (英語: テントウムシ)、ladybird (英語: テントウムシ)
165.en: Ledyba and Rediba may come from ladybug or ladybird and red.

166.ja: ladybug (英語: テントウムシ)、ladybird (英語: テントウムシ)、red (英語:赤)
166.en: Ledian and Redian may be a combination of ladybug or ladybird, red, and possibly alien or guardian.

167.ja: Itomaru may be a combination of 糸 ito (thread) and 丸 maru (circle; suffix in young boy's names).
167.en: Spinarak may be a combination of , the verb spin (to form a web from threads), or Spinne (German for spider), and arachnid. Incidentally, spinnerak is the Afrikaans word for spider web.

168.ja: Ariados may be named after Ariadne, a Cretan princess in Greek mythology associated with strings and mazes. In the myth, Ariadne gave Theseus a ball of twine before he entered the Labyrinth to slay the Minotaur, so that he may trail the strand behind him and find his route back to her. Ariados may also be a combination of arachnid (Latin for spider) and 脅す odosu (to threaten) or dos (Spanish for t

169.ja: cross (英語：十字架)、bat (英語：コウモリ)、acrobat (英語：曲芸・アクロバット）、
169.en: Crobat may be a combination of cross and bat. It may also involve (referring to its agility in the air).

170.ja: Chinchou and Chonchie may be derived from 提灯 chōchin (lantern) or 提灯鮟鱇 chōchin-ankō (footballfish).

171.en: Lanturn may be a combination of lantern and turn (as in "turn on the light").

172.ja: Pichu may be a combination of ピカピカ pikapika (onomatopoeia for sparkling), チューチュー chūchū (onomatopoeia for the squeak of a mouse), and possibly プチ puchi (petite), as well as being a contraction of Pikachu.

173.ja: Py may be a truncation of pixie.
173.en: Cleffa may be a combination of clef (musical symbol) and the first two letters of fairy or fable; fa may also be derived from the popular western musical teaching method (i.e. do, re, mi, fa, sol, la, ti).

174.ja: Pupurin may be a combination of プチ puchi (petite) and プリン (pudding; referring to its soft body).
174.en: Igglybuff may be a combination of jiggly or wiggly and (a pale, soft, flexible leather).

175.ja: Togepi and Togepy may be a combination of 刺 toge (spike) and ピヨピヨ piyopiyo (onomatopoeia for a bird chirping).

176.ja: Togetic and Togechick may be a combination of 刺 toge (spike) and chick (a baby bird).

177.ja: Naty may be derived from native.
177.en: Natu may be derived from nature.

178.ja: Natio may be a combination of native, ネイティ Naty (Natu's Japanese name), and indio (Spanish and Italian for "Native American").
178.en: Xatu is derived from xat, a sort of Native American carved pole; and possibly Natu.

179.ja: Merriep may be a combination of merry, メー mē (the sound made by sheep), or Mary and sheep.
179.en: Mareep may be a combination of Mary (from the nursery rhyme "Mary Had a Little Lamb") and sheep. Mareep is also an anagram of ampere, a unit of electric current.

180.ja: Mokoko may derive from 羊毛 yōmō (wool) and a corruption of もこもこ mokomoko (fluffy).
180.en: Flaaffy may be a combination of fluffy and baa (the sound sheep make). Aa may also be a reference to an AA battery, given Flaaffy's Electric type.

181.ja: Denryu may be derived from 電流 denryū (electric current), as well as forming a pun on 電竜 den ryū (electric dragon). It may also incorporate 電球 denkyū (light bulb), referring to its tail.
181.en: Ampharos may be a combination of (a measure of electrical current) and Φάρος pháros (Greek for lighthouse), as well as amparo (Portuguese and Spanish for aid and protection).

182.ja: Kireihana may be a combination of 綺麗 kirei (beautiful), 鈴 rei (bell), and 花 hana (flower).
182.en: Bellossom may be a combination of bell, belle or bello/bella (Spanish and Italian for beautiful) and blossom.

183.ja: Marill and Maril may be a combination of mar (Spanish and Portuguese for sea) or 丸い marui (round or circular) or 鞠 mari (ball), 瑠璃 ruri (lapis lazuli), and possibly rill (a small river or brook) and マリンブルー marine blue.

184.ja: Marilli may be a combination of 丸い marui (round or circular) or 鞠 mari (ball), 瑠璃 ruri (lapis lazuli), and possibly rill (a small river or brook) and マリンブルー marine blue.
184.en: Azumarill may be a combination of azure (a shade of blue), azul (Spanish and Portuguese for blue), or azul marino (Spanish for navy blue), mar (Spanish and Portuguese for sea) or Marill, and possibly rill (a small river or brook).

185.ja: Usokkie is derived from 嘘 uso (false) possibly in combination with 木 ki (wood or tree). It could also derive from 嘘つき usotsuki (liar).
185.en: Sudowoodo is a corruption of pseudo (false) and wood.

186.ja: Nyorotono may be a combination of ニョロニョロ nyoronyoro (onomatopoeia for wriggling) and 殿 tono (lord), as well as 殿様蛙 tonosama-gaeru ().
186.en: Politoed may be a combination of (outdated term for tadpole) and toad. It may also involve poly- (prefix for many) and toed.

187.ja: Hanecco may be a combination of 葉 ha (leaf), 跳ねる haneru (to jump or hop), or 羽 hane (wing), and 根っ子 nekko (root). Its name may also be a pun on 猫 neko (cat), given its cat-like ears and tail.
187.en: Hoppip may be a combination of hop or hippity-hop and pip (small hard seed) or pipsqueak. The names Hoppip, Skiploom, and Jumpluff are together based on the phrase "Just a hop, skip, and a jump away."

188.ja: Popocco may be a combination of 蒲公英 tanpopo (dandelion) and 根っ子 nekko (root). Its name may also be a pun on 猫 neko (cat), given its cat-like face.
188.en: Skiploom may be a combination of and or plume. The names of all stages of its evolutionary line is based on the phrase: "Just a hop, skip, and a jump away."

189.ja: Watacco may be a combination of 綿 wata (cotton) and 根っ子 nekko (root). Its name may also be a pun on 猫 neko (cat), given its cat-like face.
189.en: Jumpluff may be a combination of jump, fluff, and possibly plush. The names of all stages of its evolutionary line is based on the phrase: "Just a hop, skip, and a jump away."

190.ja: ape (英語：類人猿)、palm (英語：手のひら)
190.en: Aipom and Eipam may be a combination of ape and palm.

191.ja: Himanuts may be a combination of 向日葵 himawari (sunflower) and nuts. It may also refer to 夏 natsu (summer).
191.en: Sunkern may be a combination of sunflower and kernel.

192.ja: Kimawari may be a combination of 黄 ki (yellow) or 吉 ki (joy) and 向日葵 himawari (sunflower).
192.en: Sunflora may be a combination of sun or sunflower and flora.

193.ja: Yanma and Yanyanma derive from 蜻蜓 yanma (large dragonfly).

194.ja: Wooper and Upah may be derived from wooper looper (Japanese: ウーパールーパー ūpārūpā), a marketing term created in Japan that started a pet salamander raising fad. Wooper loopers are the Japanese term for the axolotl, a species of salamander.

195.ja: Nuoh may be a combination of 沼 numa (swamp, marsh, pond, or lake) and 王 ō (king) or オオサンショウウオ ōsanshōuo (Japanese giant salamander).
195.en: Quagsire may be a combination of and the honorific sire or (a genus of salamander).

196.ja: Eifie may be a combination of エスパー ESPer (one who has extrasensory perception) and フィール feel. It is also similar to イーブイ Eievui (Eevee's Japanese name).
196.en: Espeon may be a combination of the acronym ESP (extrasensory perception) and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eevee Evolutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name.

197.ja: Bracky may be a combination of black and lucky or 月 tsuki (moon).
197.en: Umbreon may be a combination of (darkest part of a shadow; Latin for shadow) and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eevee Evolutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name.

198.ja: Yamikarasu may be a combination of 闇 yami (darkness) and 烏 karasu (crow).
198.en: Murkrow may be a combination of murky or murder (a group of crows) and crow.

199.ja: Yadoking may be a combination of 宿借り yadokari (hermit crab), 宿主 yadonushi (parasitic host), and king. Like Slowbro, it is also partially named after the famous singer-songwriter Jadranka Stojaković, who had worked for Konami during her residence in Japan. Her name is transliterated into Japanese as ヤドランカ Yadoranka, which inspired one of her former colleagues who named Slowbro and Slowking.
199.en: Slowking may be a combination of slow and king.

200.ja: Muma may be derived from 夢魔 muma (nightmare; demon in a dream).
200.en: Misdreavus may be a combination of mischievous and dream. It may also involve mistress, mysterious, miscreant, dread, and dreary.

201.en: Unown may be derived from unknown.

202.ja: Sonans is homophonous with the interjection そうなんす Sō na-n-su, which roughly means, "That's the way it is." It is played off by its pre-Evolution, Wynaut, whose Japanese name, Sohnano, is nearly homophonous with the phrase そうなの？ Sō na no?, meaning, "Really?" Together, they make for a potential perpetual conversation of, "Really?" followed by, "That's the way it is," and so on, as seen in Camp Pikac
202.en: Wobbuffet may be a combination of wobble and buffet (to strike repeatedly). It might also incorporate a corruption of "What about that?".

203.ja: Kirinriki is a palindrome which may be derived from 麒麟 kirin (giraffe). It may also involve 念力 nenriki (telekinesis).
203.en: Girafarig may be derived from giraffe. The name is a palindrome, which may refer to its two-headed appearance.

204.ja: Kunugidama may be a combination of 椚木 kunugi (sawtooth oak) and 玉 tama (ball).
204.en: Pineco may be derived from pine cone.

205.ja: forest (英語: 森)、fortress (英語: 要塞)
205.en: Forretress and Foretos may be a combination of forest, fortress, and possibly ferro (Portuguese for steel) and turret.

206.ja: Nokocchi may be derived from an anagram of ツチノコ, the katakana spelling of 槌の子 tsuchinoko (a fabled snake-like creature).
206.en: Dunsparce may be a combination of dun (a dull, sandy brown - the colour of Dunsparce's eyes and back; or a subadult mayfly) and sparse, for its rarity.

207.ja: glide (英語: 滑空する)、gargoyle (英語: ガーゴイル)、glider (英語: グライダー)
207.en: Gligar and Gliger may be a combination of glide and gargoyle. It may also incorporate the surname Giger, as in H. R. Giger, the creator of the xenomorph alien, whose larval facehugger stage Gligar appears to draw some inspiration from.

208.ja: Haganeil may be a combination of 鋼 hagane (steel) and nail or tail.
208.en: Steelix may be a combination of steel, helix, and Onix or onyx.

209.ja: Bulu may be derived from bulldog.
209.en: Snubbull may be a combination of snub (short nose; to treat with disdain) and bulldog or bully. It may also involve snuggle.

210.ja: grand (英語: 雄大な、高貴な)、ブルドッグ、grumble (英語: ゴロゴロ鳴る、唸る)
210.en: Granbull and Granbulu may be a combination of grand, grumble, and bulldog or bully.

211.ja: Harysen may be derived from 針千本 harisenbon (porcupinefish). The Japanese name for porcupinefish, 針千本 (harisenbon), can be interpreted literally as "one thousand sewing needles".
211.en: Qwilfish may be a combination of quill (the noun, meaning porcupine or hedgehog spikes, or the verb, meaning to pierce with quills) and fish.

212.ja: Hassam may be a corruption of 剪む hasamu (to clip or snip).
212.en: Scizor may be a corruption of , or a combination of scissor and razor.

213.ja: Tsubotsubo may be derived from 壺 tsubo (jar) and 富士壺 fujitsubo ().
213.en: Shuckle may be a combination of shuck (husk) and barnacle or turtle.

214.ja: ヘラクレスオオカブト
214.en: Heracross and Heracros may be a combination of Hercules beetle (species of rhinoceros beetle named after the hero in the classical Greco-Roman mythology for its great strength), Ἡρακλῆς (divine hero in Greek mythology who lent his name to his ), and cross.

215.ja: Nyula may be a combination of 潜入 sennyū (sneaking), にゅー nyu (cat's purr), 鼬 yū (alternate reading of itachi, weasel), and 拉 ra (to carry off). It may also involve 野良 nora (stray or feral).
215.en: Sneasel may be a combination of sneaky and weasel. It may also derive from sneeze, an action associated with having a cold or being cold, or sneesl (Scots for to begin to rain or snow), both in reference to its Ice type.

216.ja: Himeguma may be a combination of 姫 hime (princess; small; prefix for small in ) or possibly 朏 hi (crescent moon) and 熊 kuma (bear).
216.en: Teddiursa may be a combination of teddy bear, a stuffed bear toy named after President Theodore Roosevelt, and ursa (Latin for bear).

217.ja: Ringuma may be a combination of ring, 輪 rin (wheel; ring), and 熊 kuma (bear).
217.en: Ursaring may be a combination of ursa (Latin for bear) and ring.

218.ja: Magmag may be a combination of magma and slug.
218.en: Slugma may be a combination of slug and magma.

219.ja: magma (英語: マグマ)、escargot (フランス語: カタツムリ)
219.en: Magcargo and Magcargot may be a combination of magma, escargot (French for snail), and cargo.

220.ja: Urimoo may be a combination of 瓜坊 uribō (wild boar piglet), หมู (Thai for pig) and ブーブー būbū (onomatopoeia for oinking).
220.en: Swinub may be a combination of swine and nub (referring to its lump-like nose) or cub, a young animal.

221.ja: Inomoo may be a combination of 猪 inoshishi (boar) and ブーブー būbū (onomatopoeia for oinking).
221.en: Piloswine may be a combination of pilose (covered with fine hair) and swine, as well as a play on pile of swine. It may also involve פיל píl (Hebrew for elephant).

222.ja: Sunnygo may be a combination of sunny and 珊瑚 sango (coral).
222.en: Corsola may be a combination of coral and sole (a piece of coral) or solar.

223.ja: Teppouo may be a combination of 鉄砲 teppō (gun) and 魚 uo (fish), which combine to form 鉄砲魚 teppō-uo (archerfish).
223.en: Remoraid may be a combination of remora and raid (rapid attack).

224.ja: Okutank may be a combination of octopus and tank ( or container for liquids).
224.en: Octillery may be a combination of octopus and artillery.

225.en: Delibird may be a combination of delivery and bird. Deli may have derived from the Adélie penguin. Pronouncing Delibird with the same inflection as delivered yields a very similar pronunciation, with the "b" and "v" being the only variation between the two words. As the "v" sound does not exist in the Japanese language, it is commonly pronounced as a "b" sound by Japanese speakers.

226.ja: manta (英語: オニイトマキエイ、マンタ)、marine (英語: 海)
226.en: Mantine and Mantain may be combinations of manta, and marine, brine (salt water), or tine (point on an implement; branch on a horn).

227.ja: Airmd may be a combination of エア air and アームド armed, which are gairaigo.
227.en: Skarmory may be a combination of sky and armory or armor. It may also involve scar.

228.ja: Delvil may be a corruption of devil.
228.en: Houndour may be a combination of hound and dour (grim, dreary, severe).

229.ja: Hellgar may be a combination of hell and gar (Old English for spear; referring to its tail). It could also refer to Garmr, a hellhound in Norse mythology.
229.en: Houndoom may be a combination of hound and doom (death or a terrible fate).

230.en: Kingdra may be a combination of king and dragon. It may also involve (a mythological water creature).

231.ja: Gomazou may be a combination of 細かい komakai (small, minute) and 象 zō (elephant).
231.en: Phanpy may be derived from elephant and possibly infant or the diminutive suffix -y.

232.ja: don (スペイン語: ドン、首領)、elephant (英語: 象)
232.en: Donphan and Donfan may be a combination of don (Spanish honorific, similar to sir) or -don (common suffix in names of prehistoric animals, e.g. mastodon, from the Ancient Greek word for tooth) and elephant.

233.en: Porygon2 is a combination of Porygon and the number 2, referencing the Pokémon's status as the second Evolution of Porygon and it being intended as an upgraded "version 2" of Porygon.

234.ja: Odoshishi may be derived from 鹿威し shishi-odoshi.
234.en: Stantler may be a combination of startle, stare, or stag (a male deer) and antler.

235.ja: Doble may be a combination of dog, daub, doodle, and double (referring to Smeargle's ability to copy moves).
235.en: Smeargle may be a combination of smear and beagle.

237.en: Hitmontop may be a combination of hit, monster, and top (a reference to spinning tops and how it fights, by spinning on its head). The name is also related to Hitmonlee and Hitmonchan, both of which have been parallel Evolutions to it since Generation II.

238.en: Smoochum may be derived from smooch (a kiss) and possibly chum (friend). It may also be a pun on "smooch 'em".

246.en: Larvitar may be derived from larva, and tar (alluding to the misconception of petroleum and tar originating from dinosaurs) or Reptar. It may also involve Tarasque..

350.ja: Milokaross may be a combination of Venus de Milo and κάλλος kállos (Greek for beauty) or (Greek goddesses of grace and beauty).
350.en: Milotic may be a combination of Venus de Milo (a sculpture known for its beauty) and aquatic, melodic, exotic, or lotic (a river ecosystem).

351.ja: Powalen may be a combination of ポワン powan (onomatopoeia of a sudden transformation, similar to "poof" in English) and 変わる kawaru (to change or transform).
351.en: Castform may be a combination of forecast (the prediction of weather) and transform or form (referring to its ability to change form depending on the weather).

352.ja: Kecleon and Kakureon may be a combination of 隠れる kakureru (to hide, to conceal oneself) and chameleon.

353.ja: Kagebouzu may be a combination of 影 kage (shadow) or 影法師 kagebōshi (silhouette) and てるてる坊主 teru teru bōzu.
353.en: Shuppet may be a combination of shadow or sheet and puppet, or a contraction of .

354.ja: Juppeta may be a combination of 呪 ju (curse, spell) and puppet. It may also refer to Geppetto, a woodcarver who built a puppet that came to life in of Pinocchio.
354.en: Banette may be a combination of bane (cause of trouble, harm, or death), and marionette.

355.ja: Yomawaru may be a combination of 妖魔 yōma (ghost), 悪 waru (bad thing), and 夜回り yomawari (night watch).
355.en: Duskull may be a combination of dusk and skull.

356.ja: Samayouru may be a combination of 彷徨う samayou (to wander about) and 夜 yoru (night).
356.en: Dusclops may be a combination of dusk and cyclops.

357.ja: トロピカル、サウルス（ラテン語：トカゲ）
357.en: Tropius may be a combination of tropical and -saurus (common suffix in names of dinosaurs and extinct reptiles derived from the Ancient Greek word for lizard). It may also involve tropism (orientation of plants in response to a particular environmental stimulus).

358.ja: Chirean may be a combination of ちりんちりん chirinchirin (the sound of a bell chiming) and 風鈴 fūrin (wind chime).
358.en: Chimecho may be a combination of chime and echo.

359.ja: Absol may be derived from absolute or absolution (setting free from guilt), as well as 危ない abunai (dangerous) and possibly 物騒 bussō (dangerous) or 恐る osoru (to fear). It may also be a combination of the Latin prefix ab- (away from) and sol (Latin for sun).

360.ja: Sohnano is homophonous with the phrase そうなの？ Sō na no?, which means "Really?" or "Is that so?" This plays off its Evolution, Wobbuffet, whose Japanese name of Sonans is homophonous with the interjection そうなんす Sō na-n-su, which roughly means, "That's the way it is." Together, they make for a potential perpetual conversation of, "Really?" followed by, "That's the way it is," and so on, as seen in Ca
360.en: Wynaut may be a play on the phrase, "Why not?"

361.en: Snorunt may be a combination of snow and runt (a smaller-than-average animal).

363.ja: Tamazarashi may be a combination of 球 tama (sphere) and 海豹 azarashi (earless seal).
363.en: Spheal may be a combination of sphere and .

365.en: Walrein may be a combination of walrus, reign, and possibly rain.

366.ja: Pearlulu may be a combination of pearl and possibly لؤلؤ lulu ( for pearls).
366.en: Clamperl may be a combination of clamp or clam and pearl.

367.ja: Huntail may be a combination of hunt, 斑点 hanten (speck or fleck), and tail.

368.ja: Sakurabyss may be a combination of 桜 sakura (cherry blossom) and abyss.

369.ja: Glanth may be a combination of 爺 jī (old man) and coelacanth.
369.en: Relicanth may be a combination of relic (something old that has survived the passage of time), , and coelacanth.

370.en: Luvdisc and Lovecus may be a combination of love and .

373.en: Salamence may be a combination of and menace, vengeance, vehemence, violence, or immense.

374.en: Beldum and Dumbber may be derived from dumbbell, a small weight used in weight training.

375.en: Metang may be a combination of metal and tang (onomatopoeic sound of metal being struck) or étang (French for the color pond blue). Metang is also an anagram of magnet.

376.en: Metagross may be a combination of metal and cross (referring to its facial feature), (referring to its weight of 1212 lbs., as a gross is 12×12), or groß (German for large).

377.ja: regis（ラテン語：王）、register (英語: レジスタ)、rock (英語: 岩)
377.en: Regirock may be a combination of regis (Latin for royal) and rock.

378.ja: regis（ラテン語：王）、register (英語: レジスタ)、ice (英語: 氷)
378.en: Regice may be a combination of regis (Latin for royal) and ice.

379.ja: regis（ラテン語：王）、register (英語: レジスタ)、steel (英語: 鋼)
379.en: Registeel may be a combination of regis (Latin for royal) and steel.

380.ja: 『クリティアス』（プラトンの著作）
380.en: Latias may be a combination of lateō (Latin for I conceal, I lie hidden) and -as (Latin feminine suffix). It might also reference Eos (the Greek goddess of dawn) or (a genus of parrots with red and blue plumage). It's possible that its name is derived from Plato's dialogue .

381.ja: 『ティマイオス』（プラトンの著作）
381.en: Latios may be a combination of lateō (Latin for I conceal, I lie hidden) and -os (Latin masculine suffix). It might also reference Eos (the Greek goddess of dawn) or (a genus of parrots with red and blue plumage). It's possible that its name is derived from Plato's dialogue .

382.ja: Kyogre is a combination of 海 kai (sea) or 海王 kaiō (king of the sea) and ogre. It may also involve orca (killer whale).

383.ja: Groudon may be a combination of ground and don (Spanish for lord) or -don (common suffix in dinosaur names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth). It may also involve 土 do (earth) or ドン don (onomatopoeia for booming or crashing sound).

384.ja: Rayquaza may be derived from 裂空 rekkū (sky-rending), a word appearing in various media and merchandise that featured Rayquaza. The latter half may be derived from 座 za (seat), relating to compounds such as 星座 seiza (constellation) and 王座 ōza (throne). It may also involve רָקִיעַ rāqī́aʿ (Hebrew for firmament), ray, and quasar.

385.ja: Jirachi may be derived from желать zhelatʹ (Russian for to wish). It may also involve jinn or 幸 sachi (Japanese for fortune).

386.en: Deoxys is derived from —or DNA—the medium of genetic code in cellular organisms.

389.ja: Dodaitose may be a combination of 土 do (earth or ground), 大 dai (large), 土台 dodai (), and tortoise.

390.ja: Hikozaru may be a combination of 火 hi (fire), 小 ko (small), 子 ko (child), and 猿 saru (monkey, ape).
390.en: Chimchar may be a combination of chimp and char (to burn).

394.ja: Pottaishi may be a combination of ぽちゃぽちゃ pochapocha (sound of a pebble splashing in water) and 皇太子 kōtaishi (crown prince).
394.en: Prinplup may be a combination of prince and plup (sound of a pebble hitting water).

395.ja: Emperte may be a combination of emperor penguin and Bonaparte (Napoleon's surname).
395.en: Empoleon may be a combination of emperor penguin, polar or South Pole, and Napoleon.

396.ja: Mukkuru may be a combination of 椋鳥 mukudori (gray starling) and クルクル kurukuru (onomatopoeia of a bird's call).
396.en: Starly may be derived from starling.

397.ja: Mukubird is a combination of 椋鳥 mukudori (gray starling) and bird.
397.en: Staravia is a combination of starling and avian (pertaining to birds).

399.ja: Bippa may be a combination of beaver and 出っ歯 deppa (protruding tooth).

400.en: Bibarel may be a combination of beaver or Biber (German for beaver) and barrel.

403.ja: Kolink may be a combination of 子 ko (child) and リンクス lynx.

404.en: Luxio may be a combination of lux (Latin for light) and leo (Latin for lion).

405.ja: Rentorar may be derived from レントゲン Röntgen (alternate name for the X-ray, named after its discoverer Wilhelm Röntgen) and 虎 tora (tiger), perhaps referencing the name "Tigris" (Latin for tiger) that Johannes Hevelius used for the constellation Lynx in his atlas.

411.en: Bastiodon may be a combination of bastion and -don (common suffix in dinosaur names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth). It may also allude to the Bastille, a French fortress that was built in the 14th century.

412.en: Burmy may be a combination of burnous (a type of cloak) and worm, with a diminutive -y.

417.ja: Pachirisu may be a combination of パチパチ pachipachi (onomatopoeia for electric crackling) and 栗鼠 risu (squirrel).

418.en: Buizel and Buoysel may be combinations of buoy and weasel.

420.ja: Cherinbo may be a combination of cherry and 桜桃 sakuranbo (cherry).
420.en: Cherubi may be a combination of cherry, cherubic (innocent or childlike), and ruby (a shade of red).

421.en: Cherrim may be a combination of cherry and cherubim or blossom.

422.en: Shellos may be a combination of shell and loss or lost (referring to its lack of a shell), or the German suffix -los (-less).

423.ja: Tritodon may be a combination of Tritoniidae (family of shell-less marine gastropod molluscs) or (marine gastropod mollusk named after , Greek god of the sea), and don (Spanish for lord) or -don (common suffix in extinct animal names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth). It may also be derived from torito (Spanish for little bull).
423.en: Gastrodon may be a combination of or Gastropteron (genus of sea slug), and don (Spanish for lord) or -don (common suffix in extinct animal names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).

424.en: Ambipom may be a combination of ambi- (prefix for both) or ambidextrous, Aipom, and palm.

425.ja: Fuwante may be a combination of ふわふわ fuwa fuwa (soft, airy, and light; or an onomatopoeia for floating), 風船 fūsen (balloon), and 不安定 fuantei (unstable).

426.ja: Fuwaride may be a combination of ふわふわ fuwa fuwa (soft, airy, and light; or an onomatopoeia for floating), 風船 fūsen (balloon), and ride, as well as a pun on 付和雷同 fuwaraidō (following others blindly).

427.ja: Mimirol may be a combination of 耳 mimi (ear) and roll (referring to its rolled-up ears).

433.en: Chingling may be a combination of chime, jingling, and -ling (common suffix to denote something that is small or immature).

434.ja: Skunpuu may be a combination of skunk and プー pū (onomatopoeia for the sound of flatulence).
434.en: Stunky may be a combination of skunk or stunk and stinky.

435.en: Skuntank and Skutank may be a combination of skunk, stank (past tense of stink), and tank.

436.en: Bronzor may be a combination of bronze and mirror.

437.zh: Bronzong may be a combination of bronze and gong (a type of musical percussion instrument), dong (onomatopoeia for deep bell reverberation), or bianzhong (an ancient Chinese musical instrument consisting of bells).

438.ja: Usohachi may be a combination of 嘘 uso (lie) or 嘘っ八 usoppachi (downright lie) and 鉢 hachi (flowerpot).

439.ja: Manene may be a combination of 真似 mane (mimicry) and ね ne (emphasis and question marker particle)—similar to Bulbasaur's Japanese etymology (fushigi da ne)—or nene (Spanish for "baby").

442.ja: Mikaruge may be a combination of 御影石 mikageishi (granite) and 軽石 karuishi (pumice). It may also involve karma.
442.en: Spiritomb may be a combination of spirit and tomb. It may also be a play on the Latin spiritum.

443.ja: Fukamaru may be a combination of 鱶 fuka (shark) and 丸 maru (round; suffix in young boy's names), as well as a pun on 深まる fukamaru (to deepen, to become deep).

444.ja: Gabite may be a combination of がぶり gaburi (with a big bite) and bite.

445.ja: Gaburias may be a combination of がぶり gaburi (with a big bite) and carcharias or アース āsu (earth).
445.en: Garchomp may be a combination of its Japanese name, carcharias (Latinization of the Greek word for shark karkharías), chomp, and possibly gargantuan.

446.ja: Gonbe may be a combination of カビゴン Kabigon (Snorlax), baby, and possibly a play on 権兵衛 Gonbē (common placeholder name for peasants in the old days).
446.en: Munchlax may be a combination of munch (to chew on food) and lax, relax, or Snorlax.

447.ja: ルカリオ、loup（フランス語：オオカミ）
447.en: Riolu is a partial anagram of Lucario, with the ca omitted.

448.ja: リュカーオーン、ゆかり（縁）
448.en: Lucario may be derived from the reverse of the Japanese transcription of orichalcum (a mythical metal) or oracle (referring to its supernatural abilities involving aura). It may also involve λύκος lúkos (Greek for wolf), and possibly luchar (Spanish for to fight).

449.en: Hippopotas is derived from hippopotamus. It may also incorporate potash, an earthy substance used as fertilizer.

450.ja: Kabaldon may be a combination of 河馬 kaba (hippopotamus) and don.
450.en: Hippowdon may be a combination of hippopotamus, power, powder (as in sand), or pow (onomatopoeia for a bodily hit), and don (Spanish for lord) or -don (common suffix in extinct animal names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).

458.ja: Tamanta may be a combination of 玉 tama (ball) and manta.

459.ja: Yukikaburi may be a combination of 雪 yuki (snow) and 頭 kaburi (head) or 被り kaburi (headdress; covering). It may also involve 蕪 kabu (turnip).
