464.ja: Dosidon may be a combination of 土 do (ground, soil), 怒 do (anger), or ど do- (prefix for very much) and サイドン Sidon (Rhydon's Japanese name).
464.en: Rhyperior may be a combination of rhinoceros, rhyolite (a type of rock, referring to its Rock type), hyperion, and superior.

465.ja: Mojumbo may be a combination of もじゃもじゃ mojamoja (shaggy or unkempt) and jumbo.
465.en: Tangrowth may be a combination of tangle and growth.

466.ja: Elekible may be a combination of electric, エレキ eleki (electric), or electricity and cable.
466.en: Electivire may be a combination of electric or electricity and wire (referring to its cord-like tails) or live wire.

467.ja: Booburn may be a combination of booby and burn.
467.en: Magmortar may be a combination of magma and (an artillery weapon).

468.ja: Togekiss may be a combination of 刺 toge (spike) and kiss, referencing its kind nature.

469.ja: Yanmega and Megayanma are a combination of 蜻蜓 yanma (large dragonfly) and the prefix mega- (great or large) or Meganeura (a genus that is a member of an order commonly referred to as "", gigantic prehistoric relatives of modern dragonflies/damselflies).

470.ja: Leafia may be derived from leaf.
470.en: Leafeon may be a combination of leaf and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eevee Evolutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name.

471.ja: Glacia may be derived from glacier. It may also involve grace.
471.en: Glaceon may be a combination of glacier (a large, slow-moving mass of ice on land) or glace (French for ice) and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eeveelutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name. Incidentally, its name is also an anagram of congeal (to become solid, usually by cooling).

472.ja: glide (英語: 滑空する)、scorpion (英語: サソリ)
472.en: Gliscor and Glion may be combinations of glide and scorpion.

473.ja: Mammoo may be a combination of mammoth and ブーブー būbū (onomatopoeia for oinking).
473.en: Mamoswine may be a combination of mammoth and swine.

474.ja: ポリゴン、Z (アルファベットの最後の文字)
474.en: Porygon-Z is a combination of Porygon and the letter Z. The latter may refer to the Z axis in a coordinate system, or the fact that it is the final stage in its evolutionary line, with Z being the final letter of the Latin alphabet. Z could also be a corruption of the number 2, referring to its status as a glitched Evolution of Porygon2.

475.ja: Erureido may be a combination of エルフ elf and/or エルボー elbow, and ブレイド blade. It may also involve 居合道 , a Japanese martial art of responding to attacks with swift swordplay.
475.en: Gallade may be a combination of gallant and blade.

476.ja: Dainose may be a combination of 大 dai (big), north, and nose. This contrasts its Mini-Noses (Japanese: チビノーズ Chibinose), whose Japanese name may be a combination of ちび chibi (small), north, and nose.
476.en: Probopass may be a combination of proboscis and compass.

477.ja: Yonoir may be a combination of 妖魔 yōma (ghost), 夜 yoru (night), or ノワール noir (black), and 悪 waru (bad, evil).
477.en: Dusknoir may be a combination of dusk and noir (French for black).

478.ja: Yukimenoko may be a combination of 雪 yuki (snow), 雪女 yuki-onna (snow woman), and 女の子 menoko (archaic word for girl).
478.en: Froslass may be a combination of frost and lass.

479.ja: In-universe, an old notebook in Rotom's Room of Platinum and Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl describes how Charon discovered and named the first known Rotom. It appeared out of a lawn mower's motor, and the person named it Rotom due to this origin.
479.en: Rotom is derived from motor spelled backwards.

480.en: Uxie and Yuxie may be a combination of U of the acronym UMA (Unidentified Mysterious Animal, wasei-eigo for cryptid) and pixie. It may also involve the pronoun you.

481.ja: Emrit may be a combination of M of the acronym UMA (Unidentified Mysterious Animal, wasei-eigo for cryptid) and spirit.
481.en: Mesprit may be a combination of M of the acronym UMA (Unidentified Mysterious Animal, wasei-eigo for cryptid) and spirit or . It may also involve esprit (liveliness of mind or spirit, and French for 'spirit') or the pronoun me.

482.ja: Agnome may be a combination of A of the acronym UMA (Unidentified Mysterious Animal, wasei-eigo for cryptid) and gnome.
482.en: Azelf may be a combination of A of the acronym UMA (Unidentified Mysterious Animal, wasei-eigo for cryptid) and elf. It may also involve the pronoun us, as well as azure.

483.ja: Dialga may be derived from diamond. It may also involve día (Spanish for day, referring to the unit of time) or dial of , sundial, and 頑 ga (tough, referring to their solid state of matter theme).

484.ja: Palkia may be derived from パール pāru (pearl), galaxia, and 海 kai (sea, ocean, referring to their liquid state of matter theme).

485.ja: Heatran may be derived from heat transfer. Its Japanese name may involve ドラゴン dragon.

486.ja: レジ（レジロック・レジアイス・レジスチルから）、γίγας（gígas、ギリシャ語：巨人）
486.en: Regigigas may be a combination of regis (Latin for royal) and gigas (Latin for giant).

487.ja: Giratina may be derived from platina (Spanish for platinum). It may also involve guilty, guillotine, ギラリ girari (onomatopoeia for glaring), and possibly girasol (a type of opal).

488.en: Cresselia may be a combination of crescent (referring to the lunar phase) and Selene (Greek goddess of the Moon).

489.en: Phione may be a combination of Manaphy and Clione (a genus of floating sea slugs).

490.ja: 真名、ephyra（英語：エフィラ、鉢クラゲ類の幼生）、mana（英語：マナ）、愛（まな）、真魚始（子供に生後初めて魚を食べさせる儀式）
490.en: Manaphy may be a combination of mana (spiritual life force in Polynesian culture) and fay (fairy).

491.ja: Darkrai may be a combination of dark and 暗い kurai (dark).

492.ja: 谢谢 (xièxie)（中国語：ありがとう）、mini（英語：小型の）
492.zh: Shaymin is from 謝謝 xièxie (Chinese for thanks). It may also be derived from 蝟 harinezumi (hedgehog) and םשמי Shamayim (Hebrew for heaven) with the latter correlating directly with Shaymin's Sky Forme.

493.ja: ἀρχή (archē)（古代ギリシャ語：始まり、アルケー）、Ζεύς (Zeus)（古代ギリシャ語：ゼウス）
493.en: Arceus may be a combination of αρχή (Ancient Greek for origin and authority) or alpha and deus (Latin for god) or Zeus (king of the gods in Greek mythology). It may also involve arcane, aureus (Latin for golden), arc, arcesso (Latin for I summon), Archeus (an aspect of the astral plane), or (the origin state of the universe in Greek mythology, often personified as the first primordial deity).

494.ja: victory（英語：勝利）、tiny（英語：とても小さい）
494.en: Victini is derived from victory, and also possibly destiny, teeny, or tiny.

495.ja: Tsutarja is a combination of 蔦 tsuta () and 蛇 ja (snake).
495.en: Snivy may be a combination of snake and ivy. It may also involve snide, sly, snark (a snide remark), snicker (a sly or snide laugh), and/or savvy.

496.ja: Janovy may be a combination of 蛇 ja (snake) and canopy or ivy.
496.en: Servine may be a combination of serpent and vine.

497.ja: Jalorda may be a combination of 蛇 ja (snake) and lord.
497.en: Serperior may be a combination of serpent, superior, and possibly warrior and emperor.

498.ja: Pokabu may be a combination of ぽかぽか pokapoka (warm) and 豚 buta (pig) or ブー bū (oink).
498.en: Tepig may be a combination of tepid (lukewarm) and pig.

499.ja: 炒（中国語：chǎo）、ブー（豚の鳴き声）
499.en: Pignite may be a combination of pig and ignite. It may also be derived from lignite, a low-grade coal.
499.zh: Chaoboo may be a combination of 超 chāo (Chinese for super) or 炒 chǎo (Chinese for ) and 豚 buta (pig) or ブー bū (oink).

500.ja: Enbuoh may be a combination of 炎 en (flame), 豚 buta (pig), 武 bu (martial arts), ブー bū (oink), 王 ō (king), and possibly ember and boar.
500.en: Emboar may be a combination of ember, emperor, and boar.

501.ja: Mijumaru may be a combination of 水 mizu (water) or 未熟 mijuku (naive or immature) and 丸 maru (round; a common suffix in male Japanese names). According to Ken Sugimori, suffixes such as -maru or -suke were considered for Oshawott's Japanese name to make it sound more typically Japanese. Miju may also refer to an otter named Mijbil, the subject of an autobiographical novel and movie called Ring of B
501.en: Oshawott may be a combination of ocean, shell, water, wash, and otter.

502.ja: Futachimaru may be a combination of 二つ futatsu (two), 太刀 tachi (long sword), and 丸 maru (round; common suffix in male Japanese names).
502.en: Dewott may be a combination of dew, water, and otter. It may also involve duel, dual (referring to the two scalchops it fights with), or duo (Italian for two, referring to its place in its evolutionary line). Notably, Helen DeWitt is the name of the author of .

503.ja: Daikenki may be a combination of 大 dai (great), 剣 ken (sword), 鬼 ki (demon), and 剣鬼 kenki (devilish swordsman).
503.en: Samurott may be a combination of samurai and otter.

504.ja: Minezumi may be a combination of 見 mi (looking) and 鼠 nezumi (mouse).
504.en: Patrat may be a combination of patrol and rat, as well as a play on pack rat.

505.ja: Miruhog may be a combination of 見る miru (to observe) and groundhog.
505.en: Watchog may be a combination of watch (to observe) and groundhog, as well as a play on , or watch out.

506.ja: Yorterrie may derive from Yorkshire terrier.
506.en: Lillipup may be a combination of lilliputian and pup.

507.ja: herd（英語：群れ）、ヨークシャーテリア
507.en: Herdier and Herderrie may be a combination of herding dog and terrier.

508.ja: Mooland may be a combination of 尨犬 mukuinu (shaggy dog), ムチムチ muchimuchi (plump), and Newfoundland or Scotland.
508.en: Stoutland may be a combination of stout (bulky, in reference to its fat figure) and (breed of large working dog) or Scotland, in reference to it being based on a Scottish terrier.

509.ja: Choroneko may be a combination of ちょろまかす choromakasu (to pilfer) and 猫 neko (cat).
509.en: Purrloin may be a combination of purr (a sound a cat makes) and purloin (to steal).

510.ja: Lepardas may be a combination of leopard and λεόπαρδος leopardós (Ancient Greek for leopard) or leopardus (Latin for leopard).
510.en: Liepard may be a combination of lie and leopard.

511.ja: Yanappu may be a combination of 柳 yanagi (willow), 野菜 yasai (vegetables), 菜 na (greens), and ape.
511.en: Pansage may be a combination of Pan (the genus of chimpanzees) and (an herb). Sage can also refer to a wise person, relating to the "three wise monkeys" motif of the elemental monkeys.

512.ja: Yanakkie may be a combination of 柳 yanagi (willow), 野菜 yasai (vegetables), 菜 na (greens), and monkey.
512.en: Simisage may be a combination of simian and (an herb). Sage can also refer to a wise person, relating to the "three wise monkeys" motif of the elemental monkeys.

513.ja: 爆（中国語；bào）、ape (英語: 類人猿)
513.en: Pansear may be a combination of Pan (the genus of chimpanzees), sear, ear or seer (relating to the "three wise monkeys" motif), and pan searing.
513.zh: Baoppu may be a combination of 爆 bào (Chinese for "to burst"; "to explode"), バースト bāsuto (burst), and ape.

514.ja: 爆（中国語：bào）、ウッキー（猿の鳴き声）
514.en: Simisear may be a combination of simian, sear, and ear (relating to the "three wise monkeys" motif).
514.zh: Baokkie may be a combination of 爆 bào (Chinese for "to burst"; "to explode"), バースト bāsuto (burst), and monkey.

515.ja: Hiyappu may be a combination of 冷や hiya (cool water) and ape.
515.en: Panpour may be a combination of Pan (the genus of chimpanzees) and pour.

516.ja: Hiyakkie may be a combination of 冷や hiya (cool water) and monkey.
516.en: Simipour may be a combination of simian and pour.

517.ja: Munna may be a combination of 夢 mu (dream) and luna (Latin for moon). It may also involve mana, 花 hana (flower), muna (Finnish for egg), or むにゃむにゃ munya munya (Japanese onomatopoeia for indistinct mumbling, as if ).

518.ja: Musharna may be a combination of ムシャムシャ mushamusha (onomatopoeia for eating), 夢 mu (dream), and luna (Latin for moon). It may also involve mana and 花 hana (flower).

519.ja: Mamepato may be a combination of 忠実 mame (diligent) or 豆 mame (prefix meaning tiny) and 鳩 hato (pigeon).
519.en: Pidove may be a combination of pigeon and dove or love (referencing the heart motif).

520.ja: Hatoboh may be a combination of 鳩 hato (pigeon) and 坊 bō (boy, monk). It may also be derived from Ihatov (also spelled イーハトーヴォ/イーハトーボ Ihatovo), a fictional ideal world mentioned in some of Kenji Miyazawa's works. The Pokédex entries in , and say, "Many people believe that, deep in the forest where Tranquill live, there is a peaceful place where there is no war," which might be a reference to Miyaz
520.en: Tranquill may be a combination of tranquil (calm or unstressed) and quill (a feather from the tail of a bird).

521.ja: Kenhallow may derive from ケンホロロ kenhororo (onomatopoeia of pheasant cries) and けんもほろろ ken-mo-hororo (curt or brusque).
521.en: Unfezant may be a combination of corruptions of unpleasant and pheasant. It may also involve unfazed.

522.ja: Shimama may be derived from 縞馬 shimauma (zebra).
522.en: Blitzle may be a combination of Blitz (German for lightning) and little.

523.ja: Zebraika may be a combination of zebra and 雷火 raika (flash of lightning).
523.en: Zebstrika may be a combination of zebra and lightning strike.

524.ja: Dangoro may be a combination of 団子 dango (Japanese dumpling) and ゴロゴロ goro goro (onomatopoeia for the sound of a boulder rolling).
524.en: Roggenrola may be derived from rock and rock and roll or rock-and-roller.

525.ja: Gantle may be a combination of 岩 gan (rock) and .
525.en: Boldore may be combination of boulder, bold (outstanding or large), and ore.

526.ja: Gigaiath may be a combination of γίγας gigas, Γαῖα (the personifying the Earth), and Goliath. It may also involve アース āsu (earth).
526.en: Gigalith may be a combination of γίγας (Greek for giant) and λίθος lithos (Greek for stone). It may also refer to megalith or monolith.

527.ja: Koromori may be a combination of 心 kokoro (heart) or コロコロ korokoro (onomatopoeia for lightly rolling) and 蝙蝠 kōmori (bat).
527.en: Woobat may be a combination of woo and bat. It may also involve wool from its woolly appearance.

528.ja: Kokoromori may be a combination of 心 kokoro (heart) and 蝙蝠 kōmori (bat).
528.en: Swoobat may be a combination of swoon or swoop and bat. Its English name containing the English name of its pre-evolved form (Woobat) mirrors how its Japanese name (Kokoromori) contains the Japanese name of its pre-evolved form (Koromori).

529.ja: Mogurew may be a combination of もぐら mogura and どりゅう doryū, which are both possible readings of 土竜 (mole). It may also involve screw or shrew.
529.en: Drilbur may be a combination of drill and burrow or .

530.ja: Doryuzu may be a combination of ドリル doriru (drill), 土竜 doryū (mole), and 渦 uzu (spiral or swirl).
530.en: Excadrill may be a combination of excavate and drill.

531.ja: Tabunne may be derived from a pun of 多分ね tabun ne (maybe) or 多聞 tabun (hearing many things). It may also involve 耳朶 mimitabu (earlobe).
531.en: Audino may be derived from audio and a pun on the phrase "I dunno."

532.ja: Dokkorer may be derived from どっこらしょ dokkorasho (heave-ho). It may also involve δοκάρι dokári (Greek for beam).
532.en: Timburr is a corruption of timber and may also derive from burly.

533.ja: Dotekkotsu may be a combination of 土木 doboku (engineering works) and 鉄骨 tekkotsu (steel frame).
533.en: Gurdurr may be derived from girder (support beam).

534.ja: Roubushin may be a combination of 老 rō (old) and 武神 bushin (warlord) or 普請 fushin (construction work/public work).
534.en: Conkeldurr may be a combination of concrete and elder. It may also involve conk, having meaning as both an especially large nose and to hit someone on the head.

535.ja: Otamaro may be a combination of お玉杓子 otamajakushi (tadpole; musical notes) and 麿 maro (affectionate suffix for young men) or 麻呂眉 maro-mayu (shaved eyebrows).
535.en: Tympole may be a combination of timpani or tympanum (both being types of drums), or tympanic membrane (eardrum; referring to the bumps on each side of its head), and tadpole.

536.ja: Gamagaru may be derived from 蝦蟇 gamagaeru (Japanese common toad).
536.en: Palpitoad may be a combination of palpitation (pulsation) and toad.

537.ja: Gamageroge may be a combination of 蟇 gama (toad) and ゲロゲロ gerogero (onomatopoeia for frog croaking).
537.en: Seismitoad may be a combination of seismic and toad.

538.ja: Nageki may be a combination of 投げる nageru (to throw), 撃 geki (strike), and 鬼 ki ().
538.en: Throh may be derived from throw.

539.ja: Dageki may be a combination of 打撃 dageki (blow, strike) and 鬼 ki ().
539.en: Sawk may be derived from sock (to punch).

540.ja: Kurumiru may be derived from お包み okurumi (swaddle).
540.en: Sewaddle may be a combination of and waddle (referring to how a baby walks) or swaddle (to wrap a baby in cloth).

541.ja: Kurumayu may be a combination of 包まる kurumaru (to be wrapped) and 繭 mayu (cocoon).
541.en: Swadloon may be a combination of swaddle (to wrap a baby in cloth) and cocoon.

542.ja: Hahakomori may be a combination of 葉 ha (leaf), 母 haha (mother), and 子守 komori (babysitting).
542.en: Leavanny is a combination of leaves and nanny. It may also derive from Levana, the ancient Roman goddess of newborn babies.

543.ja: Fushide may be a combination of 節 fushi (joint/segment) and 百足 mukade (centipede).
543.en: Venipede may be a combination of venom and centipede or millipede.

544.ja: Wheega may be a combination of wheel and 毬 iga (sphere).
544.en: Whirlipede may be a combination of whirl and centipede or millipede.

545.ja: Pendror may be derived from Scolopendra.
545.en: Scolipede may be a combination of Scolopendra (genus of centipedes) and centipede or millipede.

546.ja: Monmen may be derived from 木綿 momen (cotton).
546.en: Cottonee may be derived from cotton, cottony, cottonweed, and possibly wee (small).

547.ja: Elfuun may be a combination of elf and 風 fū (wind) or typhoon.
547.en: Whimsicott may be a combination of whimsical and cotton.

548.ja: Churine may be a combination of チューリップ chūrippu (tulip), 百合根 yurine (lily bulb), and 根 ne (root).
548.en: Petilil may be a combination of petal, petite, petunia, or , and lily or li'l (little).

549.ja: Dredear may be a combination of dress, dear, lady, and Dianthus.
549.en: Lilligant may be a combination of lily and elegant.

550.ja: Bassrao may be a combination of bass and 丈夫 masurao (warrior, referring to its ) or バカラオ bakarao (bacalhau).
550.en: Basculin may be a combination of bass and masculine.

551.ja: Meguroco may be a combination of 目黒 meguro (black-eyed) and crocodile.
551.en: Sandile may be a combination of sand, crocodile, and possibly a play on sandal (footwear).

552.ja: Waruvile may be a combination of 悪い warui (bad/evil/sinful), crocodile, and possibly gavial.
552.en: Krokorok may be a combination of crocodile, rock, and crook.

553.ja: Waruvial may be a combination of 悪い warui (bad/evil) and gavial.
553.en: Krookodile may be a combination of corruptions of crook and crocodile.

554.ja: Darumaka and Darumakka seem to be a combination of and 真赤 makka (crimson) or a corruption of macaque.

555.ja: Hihidaruma may be a combination of 火 hi (fire), 緋 hi (scarlet), 狒々 hihi (baboon), Daruma, and 火達磨 hidaruma (the state of being on fire).
555.en: Darmanitan may be a combination of (alternate spelling of daruma) and orangutan.

556.ja: Maracacchi may be a combination of maraca, cactus and mariachi.
556.en: Maractus may be a combination of maraca and cactus.

557.ja: Ishizumai may be a combination of 石 ishi (stone) and 住まい sumai (dwelling).
557.en: Dwebble may be a combination of dwell or dwelling and pebble.

558.ja: Iwapalace may be a combination of 岩 iwa (rock) and palace.
558.en: Crustle may be a combination of , crustacean, crab, and castle.

559.ja: Zuruggu may be derived from 狡 zuru (sly fellow), 狡い zurui (crafty), or ずるずる zuruzuru (onomatopoeia for dragging slowly).
559.en: Scraggy may be derived from scraggy (scrawny) or scraggly. It may also involve scrappy (feisty), saggy, and baggy.

560.ja: Zuruzukin may be a combination of 狡 zuru (sly fellow), 狡い zurui (crafty), or ずるずる zuruzuru (onomatopoeia for dragging slowly) and 頭巾 zukin (hood) or possibly skin.
560.en: Scrafty may be a combination of scrappy (feisty), scruffy, or scraggy (scrawny), and crafty (being skilled at deceit to achieve one's goals).

561.ja: Symboler may be a combination of symbol and the agentive suffix -er. It may also involve (a type of wind) or Ra (the Ancient Egyptian falcon-headed sun god).
561.en: Sigilyph may be a combination of sigil and glyph. It may also draw its name from sylph, a mythological spirit of the air.

562.ja: Yamask may be a combination of 闇 yami (darkness), や ya (bad), or Yama (lord of the dead in Hindu and Buddhist mythology), and mask.

563.ja: Desukarn may be a combination of death and 棺 kan (coffin). It may also be a pun on です か desu ka, a form used at the end of an interrogative sentence which can be translated as "Is it?".
563.en: Cofagrigus may be a combination of coffin and sarcophagus. It may also come from grim, grief, or egregious.

564.ja: Protoga may be a combination of Protostega and tortuga (Spanish for turtle or tortoise).
564.en: Tirtouga may be a combination of turtle and a corruption of tortuga (Spanish for turtle or tortoise).

565.ja: Abagoura may be a combination of 肋骨 abarabone (rib) and 甲羅 kōra (shell). It may also involve 暴れる abareru (to thrash or act violently).
565.en: Carracosta may be a combination of carapace (exoskeleton or shell of certain animal groups, such as turtles) and costa (Latin for rib). It may also involve costa (Spanish and Portuguese for coast) and accost (in the sense of assault), as well as allude to the , which contains numerous trilobite fossils.

566.ja: アーケオプテリクス (始祖鳥)、Archean (英語:太古代)
566.en: Archen may be derived from Archaeopteryx or the geological eon Archean.

567.ja: アーケオプテリクス (始祖鳥)
567.en: Archeops and Archeos may be derived from Archaeopteryx and -ops (common suffix in names of prehistoric animals derived from the Ancient Greek word for face). Archeops may also allude to Cheops, the Greek name for Egyptian pharaoh Khufu.

568.ja: Yabukuron may be a combination of 破く yabuku (to tear) or 破ける yabukeru (to get torn), ぶくぶく bukubuku (loose-fitting), and 袋 fukuro (bag).
568.en: Trubbish may be a combination of trash and rubbish.

569.ja: Dustdas may be a combination of ダスト dasuto (dust, trash, refuse) and 出す dasu (to take out).
569.en: Garbodor may be a combination of garbage and odor.

570.ja: zorro（スペイン語：キツネ）、roar（英語：吠え声）
570.en: Zorua may be a combination of zorro (Spanish for fox) and rua (Irish for red, possibly referring to its red fur; madra rua is Irish for fox).

571.ja: Zoroark may be a combination of zorro (Spanish for fox) and dark, 悪 aku (evil), or arch- (prefix for "top" or "chief").

572.ja: Chillarmy may be a combination of chinchilla and charming.
572.en: Minccino may be a combination of mini (referring to its miniature size), mink (an animal known for its fur and its use in women's clothing), or mint (pristine condition), and chinchilla or chino cloth. The suffix -ino or -cino is also a diminutive to denote something small.

573.en: Cinccino and Chillaccino may be a combination of chinchilla and chino cloth. The suffix -ino or -cino is also a diminutive to denote something small.

574.ja: Gothimu may be a combination of gothic and むちむち muchimuchi (chubby). It may also involve 予知夢 yochimu (precognitive dream).
574.en: Gothita may be derived from and the Spanish feminine diminutive suffix -ita. It may also be a contraction of .

575.ja: Gothimiru may be a combination of and 見る miru (to see).
575.en: Gothorita may be a combination of and señorita ("young woman" or the title "Miss" in Spanish), or a corruption of .

576.ja: Gothiruselle may be a combination of gothic and mademoiselle (the title "Miss" in French).
576.en: Gothitelle may be a combination of and mademoiselle (the title "Miss" in French) or stelle (Italian for "stars"). It may also involve foretell.

577.ja: Uniran may be a combination of uni- (prefix meaning one, as in unicellular organism) and 卵 ran (egg cell). It may also refer to se unirán (Spanish for "they will join together"), as Solosis appears to be made of two parts.
577.en: Solosis may be a combination of solo (singular) and meiosis or mitosis (cell division).

578.ja: Doublan may be a combination of double and 卵 ran (egg cell).
578.en: Duosion may be a combination of duo (two) and , , or .

579.ja: Lanculus may be a combination of 卵 ran (egg cell), and nucleus or homunculus.
579.en: Reuniclus may be a combination of reunite and homunculus, nucleus, or cluster.

580.ja: Koaruhie may be a combination of 子 ko (child) or 小 ko (small) and a corruption of 家鴨 ahiru (duck).
580.en: Ducklett may be a corruption of ducklet, possibly combined with wet or -ette (suffix meaning small).

581.en: Swanna is derived from swan and may be a combination of swan and ballerina.

582.ja: Vanipeti may be a combination of vanilla and petit (French for small).
582.en: Vanillite may be a combination of vanilla and lite (reduced) or possibly delight, as well as stalactite.

583.ja: Vanirich may be a combination of vanilla and rich.
583.en: Vanillish may be a combination of vanilla and delish (slang term for delicious).

584.ja: Baivanilla may be a combination of bi- (prefix meaning two) or 倍 bai (double) and vanilla.
584.en: Vanilluxe may be a combination of vanilla and deluxe.

585.ja: Shikijika may be a combination of 四季 shiki (four seasons) and 鹿 shika (deer).
585.en: Deerling may be a combination of deer and darling or yearling (young animal between the age of one to two years old), with -ling being a common suffix to denote something that is small or immature; it may also reference the 1938 novel The Yearling, about a boy who adopts an orphaned fawn.

586.ja: Mebukijika may be a combination of 芽吹き mebuki (to bud) and 鹿 shika (deer).
586.en: Sawsbuck may be a play on sawbuck (framework for holding wood to be sawn), which contains the word buck (male deer). SAWS may also be an acronym for the names of the seasons (summer, autumn, winter, and spring).

587.ja: Emolga may be a combination of 衣紋 emon (drapery) and モモンガ momonga (flying squirrel).

588.ja: Kaburumo may be derived from 蝸牛被 maimaikaburi (Carabus blaptoides) and 甲虫 kabutomushi (beetle).
588.en: Karrablast may be a combination of Carabus (genus of beetles belonging to the ground beetle family), κέρατο kérato (Greek for horn), or carapace (the upper half of a shell, which is commonly, albeit incorrectly, associated with beetles), and blast (referring to its defense mechanism) or plastron (French for chestplate). It may also be derived from Carabus blaptoides, a species of ground beetle end

589.ja: Chevargo may be a combination of chevalier and escargot.
589.en: Escavalier may be a combination of escargot (French for snail) and cavalier (French for mounted soldier) or chevalier (French for knight), as well as pun of Excalibur.

590.ja: Tamagetake may be a combination of 玉 tama (sphere), 魂消る tamageru (to be astonished), and 茸 take (mushroom).
590.en: Foongus may be a combination of fool (to deceive) and fungus. Together with , their names are a corruption of the phrase, "Fungus among us." This is confirmed by Ali the Student in North Province (Area Two) in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

591.ja: Morobareru is a combination of もろに moro ni (completely) and ばれる bareru (slang for "[for a secret] to be exposed"), referring to how it no longer convincingly looks like a Poké Ball.
591.en: Amoonguss may be a combination of amanita (a genus of mushroom) and fungus or humongous (huge). Together with , their names are a corruption of the phrase, "Fungus among us." This is confirmed by Ali the Student in North Province (Area Two) in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

592.ja: Pururill may be a combination of プルプル purupuru (onomatopoeia for jiggling) and .
592.en: Frillish may be a combination of and jellyfish.

593.ja: Burungel may be a combination of ブルンブルン burunburun (onomatopoeia for shaking) and ゲル gel.
593.en: Jellicent may be a combination of jellyfish and magnificent or translucent.

594.ja: Mamanbou may be a combination of mama and 翻車魚 manbō (ocean sunfish).
594.en: Alomomola may be a combination of aloha ( for love), mom, and Mola mola (scientific name for the ocean sunfish).

595.ja: Bachuru may be a combination of バチバチ bachibachi (onomatopoeia for electric crackling) and タランチュラ taranchura (tarantula). It may also involve bug and 虫 chū (bug).
595.en: Joltik may be a combination of jolt and tick (a parasitic arachnid).

596.ja: Dentula may be a combination of 電 den (electricity), and タランチュラ tarantula.
596.en: Galvantula may be a combination of galvanism and tarantula.

597.ja: Tesseed may be a combination of 鉄 tetsu (iron) and seed.
597.en: Ferroseed may be a combination of ferrous (containing iron) and seed.

598.ja: Nutrey may be a combination of nut and 唖鈴 arei (dumbbell).
598.en: Ferrothorn may be a combination of ferrous (containing iron) and thorn.

599.ja: Giaru may be derived from ギア gear.
599.en: Klink may be derived from clink (onomatopoeia for metal lightly striking a surface). It may also involve link or click.

600.ja: Gigiaru may be derived from ギア gear, with the first syllable repeated.
600.en: Klang may be derived from clang (onomatopoeia for metal heavily striking a surface).

601.ja: Gigigiaru may be derived from ギア gear, with the first syllable repeated twice.
601.en: Klinklang may be a combination of Klink and Klang, which in turn resemble clink and clang (onomatopoeias for metal striking a surface).

602.ja: Shibishirasu may be a combination of 痺れ shibire (numbness) or 痺鰻 shibire-unagi (electric eel) and 白子 shirasu (whitebait).
602.en: Tynamo may be a combination of tiny or tyke and dynamo.

603.ja: Shibibeel may be a combination of 痺れ shibire (numbness) or 痺鰻 shibire-unagi (electric eel), ビリビリ biribiri (onomatopoeia for an electric shock), and eel.
603.en: Eelektrik may be a combination of eel and electric.

604.ja: Shibirudon may be a combination of 痺れ shibire (numbness) or 痺鰻 shibire-unagi (electric eel) and don (Spanish for lord) or -don (common suffix in extinct animal names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).
604.en: Eelektross may be a combination of eel, electric, and possibly boss or groß (German for large).

605.ja: Ligray may be derived from little or little green men and (purported extraterrestrial beings depicted as diminutive gray-skinned humanoids).
605.en: Elgyem may be derived from the abbreviation (little green men, a stereotypical depiction of extraterrestrials as diminutive green-skinned humanoids).

606.ja: Ohbem may be a combination of 大 ō (large) and BEM (pronounced as a single word in Japanese).
606.en: Beheeyem may be a corruption of the abbreviation (bug-eyed monster, a depiction of extraterrestrials as grotesque creatures with oversized eyes).

607.ja: Hitomoshi may be derived from 火点し hi-tomoshi, which literally means "lighting a lamp" but can also refer to a person who leads a funeral procession with a torch. The name can also be interpreted as 人燃し hito-moshi (to burn a man to death).
607.en: Litwick may be a combination of lit, little, , and possibly wicked.

608.ja: Lampler may be derived from lamp.
608.en: Lampent may be a combination of lamp, lambent (softly glowing), and possibly lament.

609.ja: Chandela may be a combination of chandelier and candela (Latin for candle) or candelabra.
609.en: Chandelure may be a combination of chandelier and lure.

610.ja: Kibago may be a combination of 牙 kiba (fang or tusk), 顎 ago (jaw), and dragon.
610.en: Axew may be a combination of axe and chew or hew (to chop a material with a tool).

611.ja: Onondo may be a combination of 斧 ono (axe) dragon, and -don (common suffix in dinosaur names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).
611.en: Fraxure may be a combination of fracture and axe.

612.ja: Ononokus may be a combination of 斧 ono (axe), 戦く ononoku (to tremble), アックス akkusu (axe), and possibly -onychus (common suffix in dinosaur names derived from the Ancient Greek word for claw). It may also involve dragon.
612.en: Haxorus may be a combination of hack (to chop or cut down), axe, -saurus (common suffix in names of dinosaurs and extinct reptiles derived from the Ancient Greek word for lizard), and possibly hazardous.

613.ja: 熊、くしゅん（くしゃみの音の表現）
613.en: Cubchoo may be a combination of cub and ah-choo (sound of a sneeze).
613.zh: Kumasyun may be a combination of 熊 kuma (bear) and くしゅん kushun (onomatopoeia of a sneeze). It may also incorporate 熊 xióng (Chinese for bear).

614.ja: Tunbear may be a combination of tundra, 氷柱 tsurara (icicle), and bear.
614.en: Beartic may be a combination of bear and arctic. Notably, arctic itself derives from ἄρκτος arktos, Ancient Greek for bear. It may also incorporate the word beard, from the ice crystals around its snout giving the impression of a long beard.

615.ja: Freegeo may be a combination of freeze or frigid and geometry.
615.en: Cryogonal may be a combination of cryo- (prefix for icy cold) and hexagonal.

616.ja: Chobomaki may be a combination of おちょぼ口 ochoboguchi (puckered-up mouth) and 巻貝 makigai (snail; conch).
616.en: Shelmet may be a combination of shell and helmet.

617.ja: Agilder may be a combination of agile and rider.
617.en: Accelgor may be a combination of accelerate and escargot (French for snail).

618.ja: Maggyo may be a combination of マッド maddo (mud) and 魚 gyo (fish). It may also refer to まぐろ maguro (tuna).
618.en: Stunfisk may be a combination of stun and either fisk (fish in many Germanic languages), or fish and disk (in reference to its flat shape).

619.ja: Kojofu may be a combination of オコジョ okojo (ermine) and kung fu.
619.en: Mienfoo may be a combination of ermine and kung fu.

620.ja: Kojondo may be a combination of オコジョ okojo (ermine) and 道 dō (teachings, path), commonly used in the name of many East Asian martial arts (such as judo or taekwondo)
620.en: Mienshao may be a combination of ermine and .

621.ja: Crimgan may be a combination of crimson and dragon, 顔 gan (face), or 岩 gan (rock).
621.en: Druddigon may be a combination of dragon and ruddy (reddish in color).

622.ja: Gobit may be a combination of golem and bit (small in size) or 小人 kobito (small person).
622.en: Golett may be a combination of golem and the diminutive suffix -let.

623.ja: Goloog may be a combination of golem and サイボーグ saibōgu (cyborg).
623.en: Golurk may be a combination of golem and lurk (referring to its Ghost type).

624.ja: Komatana may be a combination of 駒 koma (a piece in the board game Shogi), 細切れ komagire (small pieces), and 刀 katana (sword) or 小刀 kogatana (knife).
624.en: Pawniard may be a combination of (the most basic piece in chess or a low-level operative in an organization) and poignard or poniard (a lightweight dagger). It may also involve palliard (beggar or thief).

625.ja: Kirikizan may be a combination of 切り刻む kirikizamu (to mince) and 斬 zan (to cut or slay).
625.en: Bisharp may be a combination of the piece in chess and sharp. It may also involve bisect (to cut or split into two).

626.ja: Buffron may be a combination of buffalo, afro, and bison.
626.en: Bouffalant may be a combination of bouffant (puffy, round hairstyle) and buffalo.

627.ja: Washibon may be a combination of 鷲 washi (eagle) or Washington and 坊 bon (boy, as a prefix or a nickname), ibon (Filipino for bird), or haribon (Philippine Eagle).
627.en: Rufflet may be a combination of rough, ruff (either referring to the or named after it), or ruffle, and eaglet (a young eagle).

628.ja: Warrgle may be a combination of war or warrior and eagle. It may also involve warble, a sound a bird makes.
628.en: Braviary may be a combination of brave or bravery and aviary. It may also involve eyrie.

629.ja: Valchai may be a combination of vulture and child.
629.en: Vullaby may be a combination of vulture and lullaby (which are often sung to children) or baby.

630.ja: Vulgina may be a combination of vulture or vulgar and regina (Latin and Italian for queen).
630.en: Mandibuzz may be a combination of mandible or mandil (Spanish for apron) and buzzard.

631.ja: Kuitaran may be a combination of 蟻食 arikui (anteater) and anteater or (a kind of furnace), and possibly a play on 食い足らん kuitaran (has not eaten enough).
631.en: Heatmor may be a combination of heat and armor or , as well as a play on eat more or heat more.

632.ja: Aiant may be a combination of アイアン aian (iron) and ant.
632.en: Durant may be a combination of durable or durus (Latin for hard) and ant.

633.ja: Monozu may be a combination of mono- (prefix meaning one) and 頭 zu (head).
633.en: Deino may be a combination of dino (short for dinosaur) and ein or eins (German for one). It may also derive from δεινός deinos (Ancient Greek for terrible), which has been used for the names of dinosaurs and other prehistoric animals such as Deinosuchus and Deinonychus. Deino is also the name of one of the Graeae, the three sisters in Greek mythology who shared one interchangeable eye, leaving tw

634.ja: Dihead may be a combination of di- (Greek prefix for two) or 二 ji (two) and head.
634.en: Zweilous may be a combination of zwei (German for two) and jealous (as the heads always fight over food) or zealous (reflecting Zweilous's voracious appetite and intensely competitive nature).

635.ja: Sazandora may be a combination of 三 san (three), 残忍 zannin (brutal) or 残酷 zankoku (cruel), and hydra. It may also incorporate dragon.
635.en: Hydreigon may be a combination of , drei (German for three), and dragon.

636.ja: Merlarva may be a combination of めらめら meramera (onomatopoeia for flaring) and larva.
636.en: Larvesta may be a combination of larva and (Roman goddess of the hearth and home, symbolised and represented by an eternally-burning fire located in her ). It may also involve lava.

637.ja: Ulgamoth may be a combination of ウルカヌス Urukanusu (), 蛾 ga (moth), and moth.
637.en: Volcarona may be a combination of volcano or (Roman god of fire and volcanoes) and , referring to its Fire type and status as the Sun Pokémon.

638.ja: コバルト、コバルトブルー
638.en: Cobalion and Cobalon may be combinations of cobalt (referring to and the color of cobalt blue) and stallion or champion.

639.en: Terrakion may be a combination of terra (Latin for earth) or terracotta (a clay-based material, as well as its respective color), takin, and champion.

640.en: Virizion may be a combination of viridian (a bluish-green color) or viridis (Latin for green) and champion.

641.ja: tornado（英語：竜巻）、Aiolos（アイオロス；ギリシア神話の風神）
641.en: Tornadus and Tornelos may be a combination of tornado and Aeolus (ruler of the winds in Greek mythology; also spelled Αἴολος Aiolos).

642.ja: Voltolos may be a combination of volt and Αἴολος Aiolos (Greek name for Aeolus).
642.en: Thundurus may be derived from thunderous and possibly Aeolus (ruler of the winds in Greek mythology).

643.ja: Reshiram is derived from 白 shiro (white). Ken Sugimori has also stated that Re was used because it was a softer sound than Zekrom's Ze, as Reshiram is a less physically powerful character.

644.ja: Zekrom is derived from 黒 kuro (black). Ken Sugimori has also stated that Ze was used because it was a harder, stronger sound than Reshiram's Re, as Zekrom is a "powerful, strong character."

645.ja: land（英語：大地）、Aiolos（ギリシャ神話の風神アイオロス）
645.en: Landorus and Landlos may be a combination of land and Aeolus (ruler of the winds in Greek mythology; also spelled Αἴολος Aiolos).

646.ja: Kyurem may be derived from 急冷 kyūrei (rapid cooling), グレイ gurei (gray), 冷 rei (cold), 零 rei (zero), or an anagram of ゆき (雪 yuki, snow), and 無 (nothing). Rem may also mimic the rom of Zekrom and the ram of Reshiram.

647.ja: Keldeo may be a combination of kelpie (a water horse from Celtic folklore), 蹴る keru (to kick), kelda (Old Norse for well or spring), and rodeo (a sport involving skills with horses, cows, and other livestock). It may also involve dio (Italian for god).

648.ja: Aria is a term now used almost exclusively to describe a self-contained piece for one voice usually with orchestral accompaniment, and is commonly used in opera.
648.en: Meloetta may be a combination of melody, pirouette (a type of movement in ballet), operetta, arietta, and the Italian feminine diminutive suffix -etta.

649.en: Genesect may be a combination of gene, genetics, genome, or genesis; and insect.

650.ja: Harimaron may be a combination of 蝟 harinezumi (hedgehog), 針 hari (needle), and marron (French for chestnut).
650.en: Chespin may be a combination of chestnut, urchin (an old term for hedgehog), pin, (name of some species of chestnuts), and spina (Latin for thorn).

651.ja: Hariborg may be a combination of 蝟 harinezumi (hedgehog), 防具 bōgu (armor), and bogue (French for chestnut shell).
651.en: Quilladin may be a combination of and paladin.

652.ja: Brigarron may be a combination of brigandine and marron (French for chestnut).
652.en: Chesnaught may be a combination of chestnut and juggernaut, knight, or dreadnought.

653.ja: Fokko may be a combination of fox, fuoco (Italian for fire), 狐 ko (fox), and 子 ko (child).
653.en: Fennekin may be a combination of and kindle or the diminutive suffix -kin.

654.ja: Tairenar may be a combination of テール tail and ルナール renard (French for fox).
654.en: Braixen may be a combination of braise (French for hot charcoal) and vixen (female fox). It may also be an allusion to bruxa (Portuguese for witch) or brazen (meaning bold).

655.ja: Mahoxy may be a combination of 魔導師 madōshi (mage), 魔法 mahō (magic), fox, and foxy.
655.en: Delphox may be a combination of and fox.

656.ja: Keromatsu is a combination of ケロケロ kerokero (onomatopoeia for a frog's croaking) and 沫 matsu (bubble or foam) or 飛沫 himatsu (spray or splash).
656.en: Froakie may be a combination of frog, froth, croak, and rookie.

657.ja: Gekogashira may be a combination of げこっ geko (croak) and 若頭 wakagashira (a young Yakuza subordinate leader) or 小頭 kogashira (head of an organizational section).
657.en: Frogadier may be a combination of frog and brigadier or grenadier.

658.ja: Gekkouga may be a combination of げこっ geko (croak) or 月光 gekkō (moonlight, in reference to a popular conception of ninja running through the night or to Greninja's Dark type) and 甲賀流 Kōga-ryū.
658.en: Greninja may be a combination of grenouille (French for frog) and ninja.

659.ja: Horubee may be a combination of 掘る horu (to dig) or hole, rabbit, and possibly wallaby or baby.
659.en: Bunnelby may be a combination of bunny and tunnel. It may also involve wallaby or baby.

660.ja: Horudo may be a combination of 掘る horu (to dig) or hole and 土 do (soil) or 兎 to (rabbit)
660.en: Diggersby may be a combination of digger and bunny. The name ending in "-by" may reference Bunnelby.

661.ja: Yayakoma may be a combination of 矢 ya (arrow), 稚児 yayako (baby), and 駒鳥 komadori (Japanese robin).
661.en: Fletchling may be a combination of fletching (fin-shaped stabilizers on arrows, typically made from feathers) and or hatchling.

662.ja: Hinoyakoma may be a combination of 火の粉 hinoko (sparks), 矢 ya (arrow), and 駒鳥 komadori (Japanese robin).
662.en: Fletchinder may be a combination of fletching and tinder or cinder.

663.ja: Fiarrow may be a combination of ファイア fire, アロー arrow, and possibly sparrow (as in sparrowhawk).
663.en: Talonflame may be a combination of talon and inflame.

664.ja: Kofukimushi may be a combination of 粉吹金亀子 kofuki-kogane (Japanese cockchafer), 粉 ko (powder), 吹き fuki (blowing), and 虫 mushi (bug).
664.en: Scatterbug may be a combination of scatter, bug, and possibly litterbug.

665.ja: Kofuurai may be a combination of 小 ko (small) or 粉 ko (powder) and 風来人 furaijin (wanderer).
665.en: Spewpa may be a combination of spew and pupa.

666.ja: Vivillon and Viviyon may be a combination of vivid and papillon (French for butterfly). It may also involve 美 bi (Japanese for beauty) or brillant (French for brilliant).

667.ja: Shishiko may be a combination of 獅子 shishi (lion) and 子 ko (child).
667.en: Litleo may be a combination of little, lit, and leo (Latin for lion).

668.ja: Kaenjishi may be a combination of 火炎 kaen (flame) and 獅子 shishi (lion).
668.en: Pyroar may be a combination of pyro- (prefix pertaining to fire) and roar.

669.ja: Flabébé may be a combination of フラワー furawā (flower) and bébé (French for baby).

670.en: Floette may be a combination of flower and floret, as well as the French diminutive suffix -ette.

671.ja: flower（英語：花）、gorgeous（英語：豪華な、ゴージャスな）
671.en: Florges may be a combination of flower or flora and gorgeous.

672.ja: Meecle may be a combination of メー mē (onomatopoeia of a goat's bleating) and vehicle or bicycle.
672.en: Skiddo may be a combination of skid and kiddo (slang for kid, which is also a term used for young goats). However, the way it is pronounced in the animated series implies it may be a corruption of skidoo.

673.ja: Gogoat may be derived from go, 轟轟 gōgō (rumbling), and goat.

674.ja: Yancham may be a combination of やんちゃ yancha (naughty) and champion.
674.en: Pancham may be a combination of panda, punch and champion.

675.ja: Pangoro and Goronda may be a combination of ごろつき gorotsuki (rogue), ごろごろ gorogoro (rumbling sound), and panda.

676.ja: Trimmien may be a combination of trimming, chien (French for dog), and possibly a play on the phrase très bien (French for excellent).
676.en: Furfrou may be a combination of fur and frou-frou (fancy, overly elaborate).

677.ja: Nyasper may be a combination of ニャー nyā (onomatopoeia of the sound cats make), and esper.
677.en: Espurr may be a combination of , esper, and purr.

678.ja: Nyaonix may be a combination of ニャー nyā or ニャーオ nyāo (meow) and onyx (a type of mineral with a pattern similar to Meowstic's), ὄνυξ ónyx (Greek for claw), , or Nyx (Greek goddess of the night).
678.en: Meowstic may be a combination of meow and mystic. It may also derive from stoic, referring to its generally emotionless facial expression.

679.ja: Hitotsuki may be a combination of 一つ hitotsu (one) and 突き tsuki (stab, lunge, or thrust) or 憑き tsuki (spirit possession).
679.en: Honedge may be a combination of hone, one, and edge.

680.ja: Nidangill may be a combination of 二 ni (two), 二段 nidan (second level, second stage), 断 dan (to sever), and 切る／斬る kiru (to cut, to slay with a blade), or kill.
680.en: Doublade may be a combination of double and blade.

681.ja: Gillgard may be a combination of Gilgamesh or gild (to cover with a layer of gold), 斬る kiru (to slay with a blade) or kill, and guard.
681.en: Aegislash may be a combination of Aegis (Zeus's shield in Greek mythology) and slash.

682.ja: Shushupu may be derived from プシュー pushū (onomatopoeia for spraying).
682.en: Spritzee may be a corruption of or derived words. It may also be a combination of spritz (a spray of liquid) and Fee (fairy in German and Dutch).

683.ja: Frefuwan may be a combination of fragrance or fresh and フワフワ fuwafuwa (light, airy, fluffy).
683.en: Aromatisse may be a combination of aromatic and finesse. It may also involve a corruption of aromatize.

684.ja: Peroppafu may be a combination of ペロペロ peropero (onomatopoeia for licking) or perro (Spanish for dog) and puff.
684.en: Swirlix may be a combination of swirl and licks. It may also involve helix.

685.ja: Peroream may be a combination of ペロペロ peropero (onomatopoeia for licking) or perro (Spanish for dog) and cream.
685.en: Slurpuff may be a combination of slurp and puff.

686.ja: Maaiika is derived from 烏賊 ika (cuttlefish or squid) and a pun on まあ，いいか Mā, iika ("Well, whatever"; "Oh well"). It may also involve 魔 ma (demon) or 真烏賊 maika (Japanese flying squid).
686.en: Inkay may be a combination of ink, inky or and okay.

687.ja: Calamanero may be a combination of calamaro (Italian for squid) and nero (Italian for black). It may also involve 絡ます karamasu (to entangle) and 魔 ma (demon).
687.en: Malamar may be a combination of mal- (Latin-derived prefix for bad), μαλάκιο malákio (Greek for mollusk), calamari, and mare (Latin for sea). It could also be a reference to the Spanish expression mala mar (literally "bad sea"), used by fishermen or sailors to describe rough sea conditions.

688.ja: Kametete may be a combination of 亀の手 kame-no-te (), tête (French for head), and 手 te (hand).
688.en: Binacle may be a combination of bi- (prefix for two) and barnacle, knuckle, and possibly binnacle (housing for a ship's compass).

689.ja: Gamenodes may be a combination of 亀の手 kame-no-te () and death.
689.en: Barbaracle may be a combination of barbaric and barnacle.

690.ja: Kuzumo may be a corruption of クサモドキ kusamodoki (pseudo-grass) or 藻屑 mokuzu (seaweed scraps).
690.en: Skrelp may be a combination of scrawny and kelp. It may also involve scrap as in seaweed scraps.

691.ja: Dramidoro may be a combination of dragon and 水綿 aomidoro (Spirogyra).
691.en: Dragalge may be a combination of dragon and algae.

692.ja: Udeppou may be a combination of 水鉄砲 mizudeppou (water gun) and 腕 ude (arm).
692.en: Clauncher may be a combination of claw and launcher.

693.ja: Bloster may be a combination of blow, lobster, and blaster. It is also an anagram of lobster.
693.en: Clawitzer may be a combination of claw and howitzer.

694.ja: Erikiteru may be a combination of elekiter or electric, 襟巻蜥蜴 erimaki tokage (), and 照る teru (to shine).
694.en: Helioptile may be a combination of ἥλιος hḗlios (Ancient Greek for sun) and reptile. It may also incorporate Ἥλιος (Helios, the god in ancient Greek religion who personifies the sun), (organism attracted to large amounts of sunlight) and coleoptile (the tip of a plant shoot).

695.ja: Elezard may be a combination of elekiter or electric, 襟巻蜥蜴 erimaki tokage (), and lézard (French for lizard).
695.en: Heliolisk may be a combination of ἥλιος hḗlios (Ancient Greek for sun), basilisk, and possibly obelisk (ancient Egyptian monument that represented a ray of sunlight piercing the clouds and hitting the ground at a ninety-degree angle). It may also incorporate Ἥλιος (Helios, the god in ancient Greek religion who personifies the sun).

696.ja: Chigoras may be a combination of 稚児 chigo (baby) and ソーラス sōrasu, a corruption of サウルス saurusu (-saurus, Greek-derived suffix for dinosaur names). It may also involve Tyrannosaurus.
696.en: Tyrunt may be a combination of Tyrannosaurus or tyrant, and runt.

697.ja: Gachigoras may be a combination of ガチガチ gachigachi (teeth chattering), ガチ gachi (serious or for real), ガチン gachin (banging noise), がっちり gacchiri (stocky), and Chigoras (Tyrunt).
697.en: Tyrantrum may be a combination of Tyrannosaurus, tyrant, and tantrum.

698.ja: Amarus may be a contraction of Amargasaurus.
698.en: Amaura may be a combination of Amargasaurus and aurora.

699.ja: Amaruruga may be derived from Amargasaurus.
699.en: Aurorus may be a combination of aurora and Amargasaurus.

700.ja: Nymphia may be derived from nymph, a class of female nature deities in Greek mythology commonly associated with fairies.
700.en: Sylveon may be a combination of sylph (elemental of air commonly identified with fairies), sylvan (an adjective used to describe wooded or rural areas), (a Roman deity of the woods and uncultivated land), and eon (an immeasurably long period of time, possibly referring to how long evolution takes naturally). Eon is a suffix all Eeveelutions share and was Eevee's English prototype name.

701.ja: Luchabull may be a combination of lucha libre and bull.
701.en: Hawlucha may be a combination of hawk and lucha libre, a form of Latin American professional wrestling.

702.ja: Dedenne may be a combination of 電気 denki (electricity), ヤマネ yamane (dormouse), and antenne (French for antenna).

703.ja: Melecie may be a combination of melée (small cut diamonds), 珍しい mezurashii (rare), and 石 ishi (stone). Melecie and Diancie share the same ending sound.
703.en: Carbink may be a combination of carbuncle (referring to and ), carbon (element diamonds are composed of), , and tink (to tinkle).

704.ja: Numera may be a combination of 滑る numeru (to be slippery) and ぬらぬら nuranura (onomatopoetic word for slimy).
704.en: Goomy may be a combination of goo and either gummy or slimy.

705.ja: The name may also be derived from the Irish word 'sliogán'—meaning shell—in reference to the shell on its back.
705.en: Sliggoo may be a combination of slick, slither, slippery, slimy, or slug, and goo.

706.ja: Numelgon may be a combination of 滑る numeru (to be slippery), gel, and dragon.
706.en: Goodra may be a combination of goo and dragon or Hydra. It may also be an inversion of Dragoon. It may also derive from good, referring to its kindly nature as opposed to the more violent dispositions of other pseudo-legendary Pokémon.

707.ja: Cleffy may be derived from clef (French for key) and fae.
707.en: Klefki may be a combination of clef (French for key), key, and possibly kleftis (Κλέφτης, Greek for thief).

708.ja: Bokurei may be derived from the readings of the kanji in 木霊 kodama, resulting in a combination of 木 boku (wood, tree) and 霊 rei (spirit, ghost).
708.en: Phantump may be a combination of phantom and stump.

709.ja: Ohrot may be a combination of 大 ō (big) and ロット rot (as in a root rot).
709.en: Trevenant may be a combination of tree, revenant, and possibly treant.

710.ja: Bakeccha may be a combination of お化け obake (ghost) and 南瓜 kabocha (pumpkin).
710.en: Pumpkaboo may be a combination of pumpkin and boo (a common word used to startle people) or .

711.ja: Pumpjin may be a combination of pumpkin and 神 (deity, spirit), 人 jin (man), or jinn.
711.en: Gourgeist may be a combination of gourd and geist.

712.ja: Kachikohru may be a combination of かちかち kachikachi (onomatopoeic word for frozen stiff), 凍る kōru (to freeze), and カチカチに凍る kachikachi ni kōru (to be frozen hard).
712.en: Bergmite may be a combination of iceberg, mite, and possibly stalagmite.

713.ja: Crebase may be a combination of crevasse and base.
713.en: Avalugg may be a combination of avalanche and lug (to carry or drag heavy objects, or a big/clumsy person).

714.ja: Onbat may be a combination of 音 on (sound), 音波 onpa (sound wave), and バット bat.
714.en: Noibat may be a combination of noise and bat.

715.ja: Onvern may be a combination of 音 on (sound), 音波 onpa (sound wave), and ワイバーン wyvern.
715.en: Noivern may be a combination of noise and wyvern.

716.ja: "Xerneas" begins with the letter X. It may be derived from cervus (Latin for deer), Cernunnos (a horned Celtic god), Ceryneian Hind, and アース āsu (earth).

717.ja: yvel (中英語: 悪)、quetzal (英語: ケツァール)、talon (英語: 爪)
717.en: "Yveltal" begins with the letter Y. It may be derived from yvel (a Middle English spelling of evil) and quetzal or talon.

718.ja: Z、ζυγωτός (zygōtos、ギリシア語: 結合する)、garde (フランス語: 監視・保護)、garðr (古ノルド語: 囲い、領域)
718.en: Zygarde begins with the letter Z, following the pattern established by Xerneas and Yveltal. It may also be derived from ζυγωτός zygōtos (Greek for joined) or zygote, garde (French for guard), and garðr (Old Norse for enclosure; realm).

719.ja: Diancie may be a combination of diamond, fancy, pixie, and 石 ishi (stone). The -cie could also come from メレシー Melecie (Carbink's Japanese name), reflecting Diancie’s status as a mutated Carbink.

720.en: Hoopa may be derived from hoop. It may also involve hoopla (something loud, attention-grabbing, and possibly misleading); or OOPArt (out-of-place artifact).

721.ja: volcano（英語：火山）、cannon（英語：キャノン砲）
721.en: Volcanion may be a combination of volcano or volcanic and lion or union (given how it is both Water-type and Fire-type). It may also involve canyon or cañón (Spanish for cannon).

722.ja: Mokuroh may be a combination of 木 moku (wood) and 梟 fukurō (owl), and メンフクロウ menfukurō (barn owl).
722.en: Rowlet may be a combination of root, round, or arrow, and owlet (a young owl).

723.ja: Fukuthrow may be a combination of 梟 fukurō (owl) and throw.
723.en: Dartrix may be a combination of dart and (a genus of owls) or tricks.

724.ja: Junaiper may be a combination of 樹 ju (tree), juniper, and sniper.
724.en: Decidueye may be a combination of deciduous, duel, and deadeye or .

725.ja: Nyabby may be a combination of ニャー nyā (meow), , and 火 hi (fire).
725.en: Litten may be a combination of lit, little, and kitten. It may also be a play on liten, Swedish for "small".

726.ja: Torracat may be a combination of torrid (very hot and dry), torrar (Portuguese and Spanish for "to roast" or "to toast"), or 虎 tora (Japanese for tiger), and cat.

727.ja: Gaogaen may be a combination of ガオー gao (the sound of a roar) and 火炎 kaen (flame).
727.en: Incineroar is a combination of incinerate and roar.

728.ja: Ashimari may be a combination of 海驢 ashika (sea lion), 鞠 mari (ball), and marine.
728.en: Popplio may be a combination of sea lion pup, pop, pōpō (Hawaiian for ball), and ʻīlio-holo-i-ka-uaua (Hawaiian for the Hawaiian monk seal; meaning "dog that runs in rough water").

729.ja: Osyamari may be a combination of オシャレ oshare (fashionable) or おしゃま oshama (precocious young girl), 鞠 mari (ball), and marine.
729.en: Brionne may come from brio (vivacity of style or performance), sea lion, brine, sissonne (a ballet step), and (a type of female water nymph or mermaid).

730.ja: Ashirene may be a combination of 海驢 ashika (sea lion) and sirène (French for mermaid or siren).
730.en: Primarina may be a combination of prima donna, marina, and ballerina.

731.ja: Tsutsukera may be a combination of 突き tsutsuki (pecking) and 啄木鳥 keratsutsuki (woodpecker).
731.en: Pikipek may be a combination of picus (Latin for woodpecker), pick, and peck.

732.ja: Kerarappa may be a combination of 啄木鳥 keratsutsuki (woodpecker) and 喇叭 rappa (trumpet).
732.en: Trumbeak may be a combination of trumpet and beak.

733.ja: Dodekabashi may be a combination of どでかい dodekai (huge) and 大嘴 ōhashi (toucan).
733.en: Toucannon may be a combination of toucan and cannon.

734.ja: Yungoos and Youngoose may be a combination of young or ヤンキー yankī (a certain style of juvenile delinquent) and mongoose.

735.ja: Dekagoose may be a combination of でか deka (slang term for detective) and mongoose.
735.en: Gumshoos may be a combination of gumshoe (detective) and mongoose. It may also involve gumption.

736.ja: Agojimushi may be a combination of 顎 ago (jaw) and 地虫 jimushi (grub).
736.en: Grubbin may be a combination of grub and grubbing (to scavenge or dig). Given its resemblance to an electrical plug, it may also be a play on the phrase "plug in".

737.ja: Dendimushi may be a combination of 電池 denchi (battery) and 地虫 jimushi (grub).
737.en: Charjabug may be a combination of charge (as in to charge a battery) or charger and bug.

738.ja: Kuwagannon may be a combination of 鍬形虫 kuwagatamushi (stag beetle) and cannon.
738.en: Vikavolt may come from vise grip and volt.

739.ja: Makenkani may be a combination of 負けん気 makenki (hates to lose, competitive spirit), 喧嘩 kenka (fight), and 蟹 kani (crab).
739.en: Crabrawler may be a combination of crab and brawler.

740.ja: Kekenkani may be a combination of 毛蟹 kegani (horsehair crab) and 喧嘩 kenka (fight).
740.en: Crabominable may be a combination of crab and abominable snowman.

741.ja: Odoridori may be a combination of 踊り odori (dance), 鳥 tori (bird), and 色とりどり irotoridori (varicolored).
741.en: Oricorio may be a combination of and choreography. It may also incorporate oratorio.

742.ja: Abuly may be a combination of 吊虻 tsuriabu (bee fly), lovely, and フェアリー fairy.
742.en: Cutiefly may be a combination of cutie and fly (including bee fly, fairyfly, butterfly, etc.), and a play on the phrase "cutie pie".

743.ja: Aburibbon may be a combination of 吊虻 tsuriabu (bee fly) and ribbon.
743.en: Ribombee may be a combination of ribbon, Bombyliidae (family of bee flies), and bee. It may also involve rimbombante (Spanish word for flamboyant) or bombinate (to buzz; hum).

744.ja: Iwanko may be a combination of 岩 iwa (rock), ワン wan (onomatopoetic word for barking), わんこ wanko (doggy), and 子 ko (young animal).
744.en: Rockruff may be a combination of rock, ruff (onomatopoetic word for barking, or the fur around its neck), ruffle (referring to its collar's shape) and rough.

745.ja: Lugarugan may be a combination of rougarou, loup-garou (French for werewolf), ガルル garuru (a dog's growling), and 岩 gan (rock).
745.en: Lycanroc may be a combination of and rock.

746.ja: Wishiwashi may be a combination of wishy-washy, describing the weak state of its Solo Form; wash, in reference to its Water type; and 鰯 iwashi (Japanese for sardine).

747.ja: Hidoide may be a combination of 酷い hidoi (cruel) and 海星 hitode (sea star).
747.en: Mareanie may be a combination of marine and meanie.

748.ja: Dohidoide may be a combination of ど酷い dohidoi (very cruel) and 海星 hitode (sea star).
748.en: Toxapex may be a combination of toxic and apex (as in "apex predator").

749.ja: Dorobanko may be a combination of 泥 doro (mud), 驢馬 roba (donkey), 輓馬 banba (draft horse), and 子 ko (young animal).
749.en: Mudbray may be a combination of mud and bray (cry of a donkey).

750.ja: Banbadoro may be a combination of 輓馬 banba (draft horse) and 泥 doro (mud).
750.en: Mudsdale may be a combination of mud and .

751.ja: Shizukumo may be a combination of 滴 shizuku (drop, of water or other liquids), 蜘蛛 kumo (spider), and 水蜘蛛 mizugumo (diving bell spider).
751.en: Dewpider may be a combination of dew and spider, or refer to .

752.ja: Onishizukumo may be a combination of 鬼蜘蛛 onigumo (Araneus ventricosus), 鬼 oni (demon), 滴 shizuku (drop, of water or other liquids), 水蜘蛛 mizugumo (diving bell spider), 蜘蛛 kumo (spider), and Shizukumo (Dewpider's Japanese name).
752.en: Araquanid may be a combination of arachnid and aqua.

753.ja: Karikiri may be a combination of 仮 kari (pseudo-), 刈 kari (prune; trim), and 螳螂 kamakiri (praying mantis).
753.en: Fomantis may be a combination of faux (French for both false and scythe) and mantis.

754.ja: Lalantes may be a combination of 蘭 ran (orchid) and a corruption of mantis.
754.en: Lurantis may be a combination of lure, allure, fleur (French for flower), and mantis.

755.ja: Nemasyu may be a combination of 寝 ne (sleep) and mushroom. It could also come from 寝ます nemasu (I'm going to sleep), with the su ending particle being corrupted into shu to indicate drowsiness.
755.en: Morelull may be a combination of and lull.

756.ja: Mashade may be a combination of mushroom and shade (as in lampshade).
756.en: Shiinotic may be a combination of shiitake, shine, and hypnotic.

757.ja: Yatoumori may be a combination of 夜盗 yatō (burglar) and 井守 imori (newt).
757.en: Salandit may be a combination of salamander and bandit.

758.ja: Ennewt may be a combination of 炎 en (flame) or 艶 en (bewitching) and newt.
758.en: Salazzle may be a combination of salamander and dazzle or sizzle.

759.ja: Nuikoguma may be a combination of ぬいぐるみ nuigurumi (stuffed animal) and 子熊 koguma (bear cub).
759.en: Stufful may be derived from stuffed animal or a combination of stuff and full. It may also incorporate scuffle, for its aggressive nature and propensity to fight.

760.ja: Kiteruguma may be a combination of 着てる kiteru (wearing), 着ぐるみ kigurumi (costume), and 熊 kuma (bear).
760.en: Bewear may be a combination of beware, wear, and bear.

761.ja: Amakaji may be a combination of 甘い amai (sweet), 果実 kajitsu (fruit) and 児 ji (child).
761.en: Bounsweet may be a combination of bounce and sweet.

762.ja: Amamaiko may be a combination of 甘い amai (sweet) and 舞子 maiko (dancing girl).
762.en: Steenee may be a combination of mangosteen and teeny or teenager. It may also incorporate the plant-based sweetener stevia.

763.ja: Amajo may be a combination of 甘い amai (sweet) and 女王 joō (queen, also Japanese slang for dominatrix).
763.en: Tsareena may be a combination of tsarina (a Russian female monarch) and mangosteen. It may also incorporate queen.

764.ja: Cuwawa may be a combination of cure, cute, 輪 wa (ring), and フラワー furawā (flower).
764.en: Comfey may be a combination of comfy, comfort, comfrey, , and lei.

765.ja: Yareyuutan may be a combination of やれ yare ("Do it") and オランウータン oran'ūtan (orangutan).
765.en: Oranguru may be a combination of orangutan and guru.

766.ja: Nagetukesaru may be a combination of 投げつける nagetsukeru (to throw) and 猿 saru (monkey).
766.en: Passimian may be a combination of pass and simian. It may also be a play on prosimian.

767.ja: Kosokumushi may be a combination of 小 ko (small), 足 soku (feet), or 姑息 kosoku (cowardly; underhanded), and 虫 mushi (bug).
767.en: Wimpod may be a combination of wimp and the taxonomic term pod (foot), such as in "".

768.ja: Gusokumusha may be a combination of 大王具足虫 daiō-gusokumushi (giant isopod), 具足 gusoku (fully equipped), and 武者 musha (warrior).
768.en: Golisopod may be a combination of Goliath and isopod.

769.ja: Sunaba may derive from 砂場 sunaba (sandbox) or 砂 suna (sand) and bar or いないいないばあ inai-inai-baa (peekaboo).
769.en: Sandygast may be a combination of sandy and ghast (evil spirit).

770.ja: Sirodethna may be read literally as 城ですな shiro desu na (That's a castle, huh). This may also be a combination of 城 shiro (castle), death, and 砂 suna (sand).
770.en: Palossand may be a combination of palace, loss, and sand, as well as a pun on pile of sand. It could also derive from pala (Spanish for shovel) or involve colossal.

771.ja: Namakobushi may be a combination of 海鼠 namako (sea cucumber) and 拳 kobushi (fist).
771.en: Pyukumuku may be a combination of puke, cucumber, and mucus.

772.ja: Type: Full is literally type and full (everything).
772.en: Type: Null is literally type and null (nothing).

773.ja: Silvady may be a combination of silver and buddy.
773.en: Silvally may be a combination of silver and ally.

774.ja: Meteno may be a combination of meteor and nano.
774.en: Minior may be derived from meteor. It may also involve any mixture of the words mini, minor, junior, armor, ore, and core.

775.ja: Nekkoara may be a combination of 根っ子 nekko (root; tree stump), 寝 ne (sleep), 子 ko (young animal), and koala.
775.en: Komala may be a combination of coma and koala.

776.ja: Bakugames may be a combination of 爆発 bakuhatsu (explosion) and 亀 kame (turtle).
776.en: Turtonator may be a combination of turtle and detonator.

777.ja: Togedemaru may be a combination of 棘 toge (thorn, spike) or 棘鼠 togenezumi (), 電気 denki (electricity), and 丸 maru (round), as well as a play on 棘で丸 toge de maru ("spiny and round").

778.ja: Mimikyu and Mimikkyu may be derived from mimic and きゅうきゅう kyūkyū (onomatopoeia for squeaking, similar to Pikachu). It may also involve a corruption of 迷宮 meikyuu (mystery).

779.ja: Hagigishiri may be a combination of 歯 ha (tooth), カワハギ kawahagi (filefish), ぎっしり gisshiri (densely packed), and 歯軋り hagishiri (bruxism).
779.en: Bruxish may be a combination of bruxism and fish.

780.en: Drampa may be a combination of dragon and grampa (a variation on grandpa).
780.zh: Jijilong may be a combination of 爺 jijī (an informal term for an old man) and 龍 Lóng (Chinese dragon).

781.ja: Dadarin may be a combination of 祟り tatari (cursed) and 舵輪 darin (rudder wheel).
781.en: Dhelmise may be a combination of demise and (steering mechanism of a ship).

782.ja: Jyarako may derive from ジャラジャラ jarajara (onomatopoetic sound for jingling), draco (Latin for dragon) and possibly 子 ko (young animal).
782.en: Jangmo-o may derive from jangling and moʻo (Hawaiian for lizard or dragon).

783.ja: Jyarango may derive from ジャラジャラ jarajara (onomatopoetic sound for jingling) and dragon.
783.en: Hakamo-o may be a combination of Haka (a type of war dance present across various cultures that involves vigorous movements and foot stamping) and mo'o (Hawaiian for lizard or dragon).

784.ja: Jyararanga may derive from ジャラジャラ jarajara (onomatopoetic sound for jingling), dragon, and ガランガラン garangaran (onomatopoetic sound for clanging).
784.en: Kommo-o may be a combination of commotion (in reference to the noises produced by its scales) and mo'o (Hawaiian for lizard or dragon). It may also incorporate combat, commander, and komodo as in the Komodo dragon, and the Kokomo community on the island of Maui, Maui Community, Hawaii.

785.ja: Kapu-Kokeko may be a combination of kapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and コケコッコー kokekokkō (Japanese onomatopoeia for a rooster's crowing).
785.en: Tapu Koko may be a combination of tapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and kokō (Hawaiian onomatopoeia for a rooster's crowing). It may also refer to the community on the island of Maui, Maui County, Hawaii.

786.ja: Kapu-Tetefu may be a combination of kapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and てふてふ tefutefu (an butterfly).
786.en: Tapu Lele may be a combination of tapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and pulelehua (Hawaiian for butterfly).

787.ja: Kapu-Bulul may be a combination of kapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and bull.
787.en: Tapu Bulu may be a combination of tapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and bull.

788.ja: Kapu-Rehire may be a combination of kapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and 鰭 hire (fin).
788.en: Tapu Fini may be a combination of tapu (Hawaiian for sacred or holy) and fin. It may also derive from finish or final, referencing the fact that the Ruins of Hope are where "life ends its journey", and also, possibly, that Poni Island is the final island in the island challenge.

789.ja: κόσμος（ギリシャ語: 秩序）、cosmos（英語：宇宙）、smog（英語：スモッグ）
789.en: Cosmog may be a combination of cosmos or cosmogony, and fog or smog. In-universe, a professor working at the Aether Foundation named the Pokémon.

790.ja: Cosmovum may be a combination of cosmos and ovum.
790.en: Cosmoem may be a combination of cosmos, ovum (Latin for egg), and embryo.

791.ja: sol（ラテン語：太陽）、牙（が）、がおー（ライオンの鳴き声）、leo（ラテン語：ライオン）、ガリレオ（イタリアの天文学者）
791.en: Solgaleo may be a combination of sol (Latin for sun) and leo (Latin for lion). It may also incorporate , an Italian astronomer who presented evidence that the Earth rotated around the Sun and advocated Copernican heliocentrism.

792.ja: luna（ラテン語：月）、ala（ラテン語：翼）
792.en: Lunala may be a combination of luna (Latin for moon), ala (Latin for wing), and possibly lunula (which means something crescent-shaped).

793.ja: Uturoid may be a combination of 虚ろ utsuro (void), void, and (psychic apparatus that is the unconscious source of bodily needs, impulses, and desires). It may also involve 鬱 utsu (), 美しさ utsukushisa (beauty), 移ろい utsuroi (transience), the suffix (meaning "of similar form").
793.en: Nihilego may be a combination of nihil (Latin for "indefinite nothing") and ego (Latin for "I"), which together roughly translates to "I am nothing" or "I have nothing". may also refer to the part of the psychic apparatus that governs rational decision-making, and together with nihil reflects how the creature may have no ego, and only have an id (the survival and instinctual part of the psyche). The Japanese name for porcupinefish, 針千本 (harisenbon), can be interpreted literally as "one thousand sewing needles". The last character in 針千本 harisenbon, 本, is a counter for slender objects.
