905.ja: Lovetolos may be a combination of love and Αἴολος Aiolos (Greek name for Aeolus). It can also be taken to mean ラブとロス rabu to rosu (love and loss).
905.en: Enamorus may be a combination of enamor, amorous, and Aeolus (ruler of the winds in Greek mythology).

906.ja: Nyahoja may be a combination of ニャー nyā (the sound a cat makes) and hoja (Spanish for leaf). It may also involve 葉 ha (leaf).
906.en: Sprigatito may be a combination of sprig and gatito (Spanish for kitten).

907.ja: Nyarote may be a combination of ニャー nyā (onomatopoeia for meowing) and brote (Spanish for sprout or plant bud).
907.en: Floragato may be a combination of flora or flor (Spanish and Portuguese for flower) and gato (Spanish and Portuguese for cat).

908.ja: Masquernya may be a combination of masquerade and ニャー nyā (onomatopoeia for meowing).
908.en: Meowscarada may be a combination of meow and mascarada (Spanish and Portuguese for masquerade).

909.ja: Hogator may be a combination of 火 ho (fire), ほげー hogē (onomatopoeia for being dumbfounded), fogata (Spanish for campfire) or hoguera (Spanish for bonfire), and . It may also involve (type of chili pepper) or (grains of paradise; a spice).
909.en: Fuecoco may be a combination of fuego (Spanish for fire) and cocodrilo (Spanish for crocodile). It may also come from fuocoso (Italian for fiery, especially relating to music).

910.ja: Achigator may be a combination of あちち achichi ("hot!"), mariachi, and alligator.
910.en: Crocalor may be a combination of crocodile and calor (Spanish and Portuguese for heat). It may also involve vocal or coloratura (an ornamentation of vocal melody).

911.ja: Laudbon may be a combination of laúd (Spanish lute, a musical instrument), loud, and bone.
911.en: Skeledirge may be a combination of skeleton and dirge (a song or hymn performed in mourning, such as during funerals).

912.ja: Kuwassu may be derived from クワック kuwakku (quack) and an anagram of ワックス wakkusu (wax).
912.en: Quaxly may be a combination of quack, wax (referring to hair wax), and duckling.

913.ja: Welkamo may be a combination of well or swell and 鴨 kamo (duck). It may also involve well combed.
913.en: Quaxwell may be a combination of quack, aqua, wax (referring to hair wax), well, swell (series of ocean waves) and the name Maxwell.

914.ja: Wanival may be a combination of wave and carnival.
914.en: Quaquaval may be a combination of quá-quá (Spanish and Portuguese onomatopoeia for duck sounds), aqua, and carnaval (Spanish and Portuguese for carnival). The name also appears to draw from the adjective quaquaversal, referring to a pattern that radiates in all directions from a common center; such is the case for the water feathers that fan out from Quaquaval's tail during battle.

915.ja: Gourton may be a combination of gourmet, glutton, and 豚 ton (pig).
915.en: Lechonk may be a combination of lechón (Spanish for suckling piglet), oink (onomatopoeia for a sound pigs make), and possibly chonk (slang term for a fat or large animal).

916.ja: Perfuton may be a combination of perfume and 豚 ton (pig).
916.en: Oinkologne may be a combination of oink (the sound a pig makes) and . It may also involve , a type of pork meat.

917.ja: Tamantula may be a combination of 球 tama (sphere) and tarantula.
917.en: Tarountula may be a combination of tarantula and round.

918.ja: Wanaider may be a combination of 罠 wana (trap), spider, and Schneider (German for "tailor" and "long-legged arachnid").
918.en: Spidops may be a combination of spider, spy, ops (abbreviation of "operations"), and Deinopis (genus of the ogre-faced spider).

919.ja: Mamebatta may be a combination of 豆 mame (prefix for small) and 飛蝗 batta (locust; grasshopper).
919.en: Nymble may be a combination of (immature form of an insect) and nimble.

920.ja: Exleg may be a combination of extreme, expert, or exoskeleton, and leg.
920.en: Lokix may be a combination of locust and kicks or crickets, or a pun on low kicks.

921.ja: Pawmi may be a combination of paw, palm, and mini or 鼠 nezumi (Japanese for mouse).

922.ja: Pamot may be a combination of パモ Pamo (Pawmi) and marmot.
922.en: Pawmo may be a combination of Pawmi, marmot, and dynamo.

923.ja: Parmot may be a combination of パモ Pamo (Pawmi) or パモット Pamot (Pawmo) and marmot.
923.en: Pawmot may be a combination of Pawmi or Pawmo and marmot.

924.ja: Wakkanezumi may be a combination of 輪っか wakka (loop) and 鼠 nezumi (mouse).
924.en: Tandemaus may be a combination of tandem and Maus (German for mouse).

925.ja: Ikkanezumi may be a combination of 一家 ikka (family) and 鼠 nezumi (mouse).
925.en: Maushold may be a combination of Maus (German for mouse) and household. It may also be a pun on mouse hole.

926.ja: Pupimocchi may be a combination of puppy and もっちり mocchiri (doughy, springy) or 餅 mochi (Japanese rice cake).
926.en: Fidough may be a combination of Fido (Spanish/Italian for "loyal", also a stereotypical name for a pet dog), filo, and dough.

927.ja: Bowtzel may be a combination of bow wow (onomatopoeia for a dog's bark) and pretzel.
927.en: Dachsbun may be a combination of dachshund and bun.

928.ja: Minive may be a combination of mini and olive.
928.en: Smoliv may be a combination of small or smol (diminutive spelling of small) and olive.

929.ja: Olinyo may be a combination of olive and aliño (Spanish for condiment or salad dressing) or the Portuguese diminutive suffix -inho.
929.en: Dolliv may be a combination of doll and olive.

930.ja: Oliva may be derived from oliva or a combination of olive and diva.
930.en: Arboliva may be a combination of arbor or árbol (Spanish for tree), olive or oliva (olive in various Romance languages), and diva.

931.ja: Ikirinko may be a combination of イキり ikiri (slang for a cocky person) and 鸚哥 inko (parrot).
931.en: Squawkabilly may be a combination of squawk, bill (beak), and rockabilly.

932.ja: Kojio may be a combination of 小 ko (small) or 子 ko (child), 塩 shio (salt), and geo- (prefix for earth).
932.en: Nacli may be a combination of NaCl (chemical formula for sodium chloride; table salt) and little or salty. The -i at the end of its name may also refer to iodine, a common element added to salt to prevent iodine deficiency.

933.ja: Jiodumu may be a combination of 塩 shio (salt), geo- (prefix for earth) or geode, and 積む tsumu (to stack).
933.en: Naclstack may be a combination of NaCl (chemical formula for sodium chloride; table salt) and stack, possibly in reference to (method of stone structure building that doesn't use mortar).

934.ja: Kyojiohn may be a combination of 巨人 kyojin (giant), 塩 shio (salt), and geo- (prefix for earth).
934.en: Garganacl may be a combination of gargantuan and NaCl (chemical formula for sodium chloride; table salt). It may also borrow from tabernacle.

935.ja: Carbou may be a combination of carbón (Spanish for "charcoal"), carbon (main constituent element of charcoal) and 坊 bō (boy).
935.en: Charcadet may be a combination of char (to burn), charcoal, and cadet.

936.ja: Gurenarma may be a combination of 紅蓮 guren (crimson) and arma, armadura, or armatura.
936.en: Armarouge may be a combination of arma (Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian for weapon), armadura (Spanish and Portuguese for armor) or armatura (Italian for armor), and rouge (French for red).

937.ja: Soublades may be a combination of 蒼 sō (azure) or soul and blades. It may also involve blaze or レイス reisu (wraith).
937.en: Ceruledge may be a combination of cerulean (a shade of dark blue), cruel, and edge.

938.ja: Zupika may be a combination of 頭 zu (head) and ピカピカ pikapika (onomatopoeia for sparkling).
938.en: Tadbulb may be a combination of tadpole and light bulb.

939.ja: Harabarie may be a combination of 腹 hara (belly) and バリバリ baribari (onomatopoeia for crackling).
939.en: Bellibolt may be a combination of belly and bolt.

940.ja: Kaiden may be a combination of 海 kai (sea), 海燕 kaien (northern storm petrel), and 雷電 raiden (thunder and lightning).
940.en: Wattrel may be a combination of watt and petrel.

941.ja: Taikaiden may be a combination of 大 tai (big; great), 大海 taikai (ocean), 海燕 kaien (northern storm petrel), カイデン Kaiden (Wattrel), 雷電 raiden (thunder and lightning), and 帯電 taiden (electrification).
941.en: Kilowattrel may be a combination of kilowatt and petrel; it can also be deconstructed as "kilo-" + Wattrel.

942.ja: Orachifu may be a combination of オラつく oratsuku (slang word to describe a rude or irreverent person) and マスチフ masuchifu (mastiff).
942.en: Maschiff may be a combination of mastiff and mischief.

943.ja: Mafitiff may be a combination of mafia and mastiff.
943.en: Mabosstiff may be a combination of mafia, mob, boss, and mastiff.

944.ja: Shirushrew may be a combination of 汁 shiru (juice), しるし shirushi (mark, sign), shrew, and シュルシュル shurushuru (onomatopoeia for rustling or slithering).
944.en: Shroodle may be a combination of shrew (insectivore mammal), shrewd, and doodle.

945.ja: Taggingru may be a combination of (form of graffiti signature) and 猿 saru (monkey). It may also involve ジャングル janguru (jungle).
945.en: Grafaiai may be a combination of graffiti and aye-aye. It may also be derived from Urdaibai (natural reserve home to the painted Oma forest).

946.ja: Anokusa may be derived from the phrase あの草 ano kusa (that grass) and 野草 nogusa (field grass).
946.en: Bramblin may be a combination of bramble and rambling, goblin, or gremlin.

947.ja: Anohoragusa may be derived from the phrase あのほら草 ano hora kusa ("Um... Look! Grass") and horror.
947.en: Brambleghast may be a combination of bramble and ghastly, ghast (a name used for various types of undead in fantasy fiction), or aghast.

948.ja: Nonokurage may be a combination of 野 no (field), 木耳 kikurage (), and メノクラゲ Menokurage (Tentacool).
948.en: Toedscool may be a combination of toadstool (another name for mushroom), toed (having toes), and Tentacool.

949.ja: Rikukurage may be a combination of 陸 riku (land), 木耳 kikurage (), and ドククラゲ Dokukurage (Tentacruel).
949.en: Toedscruel may be a combination of toadstool (another name for mushroom), toed (having toes), and Tentacruel.

950.ja: Gakegani may be a combination of 崖 gake (cliff) and 蟹 kani (crab) or 毛蟹 kegani (horsehair crab).
950.en: Klawf may be a combination of claw and cliff.

951.ja: Capsaiji may be a combination of capsaicin, kappa, and 児 ji (child).
951.en: Capsakid may be a combination of capsaicin (active component of chili peppers) or Capsicum (the genus of chili peppers), kappa, and kid.

952.ja: スコヴィル値、ヴィラン (villainに由来する音訳借用)
952.en: Scovillain may be a combination of Scoville (as in the Scoville scale, the measurement scale for chili pepper pungency) and villain.

953.ja: Shigaroko may be derived from a partial backward spelling of 糞転がし funkorogashi (colloquial term for dung beetle).
953.en: Rellor may be derived from roller spelled backwards.

954.ja: Beracas may be derived from スカラベ sukarabe (scarab) spelled in reverse.
954.en: Rabsca may be derived from scarab with the syllables transposed. It may also pull from Ra, the Egyptian sun god sometimes associated with scarabs.

955.ja: Hirahina may be a combination of ひらひら hirahira (onomatopoetic word for fluttering) and 雛 hina (chick).
955.en: Flittle may be a combination of flitter (a fluttering movement) and little.

956.ja: Cuespatra may be a combination of cuello (Spanish for neck), ESP, and Cleopatra.
956.en: Espathra may be a combination of , telepath, and Cleopatra.

957.ja: Kanuchan may be a combination of 鍛冶 kanuchi (blacksmith) and the Japanese diminutive suffix ～ちゃん -chan.
957.en: Tinkatink may be a combination of tinker (itinerant tinsmith) and pink or tink (sharp metallic sound).

958.ja: Nakanuchan may be a combination of 中 naka (middle), 鍛冶 kanuchi (blacksmith), and the Japanese diminutive suffix ～ちゃん -chan.
958.en: Tinkatuff may be a combination of tinker (itinerant tinsmith) and tough.

959.ja: Dekanuchan may be a combination of でかい dekai (huge), 鍛冶 kanuchi (blacksmith), and the Japanese diminutive suffix ～ちゃん -chan.
959.en: Tinkaton may be a combination of tinker (itinerant tinsmith) and ton or gigaton.

960.ja: Umidigda may be a combination of 海 umi (sea) and ディグダ Digda (Diglett).
960.en: Wiglett may be a combination of wiggle, water or white, and Diglett.

961.ja: Umitrio may be a combination of 海 umi (sea) and Dugtrio.
961.en: Wugtrio may be a combination of Wiglett, water, or wug (a word used to describe any creature with worm-like features) and Dugtrio.

962.ja: Otoshidori may be a combination of 落とし otoshi (drop) and 鳥 tori (bird).
962.en: Bombirdier may be a combination of bombardier and bird.

963.ja: Namiiruka may be a combination of 波 nami (wave), 並み nami (ordinary), and 海豚 iruka (dolphin).
963.en: Finizen may be a combination of fin, delfín (Spanish for dolphin), and citizen or denizen.

964.ja: Irukaman may be a combination of 海豚 iruka (dolphin) and man, which is often used as a suffix for superhero codenames.
964.en: Palafin may be a combination of paladin or pal (since another player is needed to evolve it) and fin or delfín (Spanish for dolphin).

965.ja: Buroron may be a combination of ぶろろろ burororo (onomatopoeia for engine sounds) and ぶるん burun (vroom).
965.en: Varoom may be derived from vroom (onomatopoetic sound of engine revving up).

966.ja: Burororoom may be a combination of ぶろろろ burororo (onomatopoeia for engine sounds) and vroom. It may also involve roam.
966.en: Revavroom may be a combination of rev (to increase the running speed of an engine by pressing on the accelerator) and vroom (onomatopoetic sound of engine revving up).

967.ja: Mototokage may be a combination of moto (Spanish, Italian, and Portuguese for motorcycle) and 蜥蜴 tokage (lizard).
967.en: Cyclizar may be a combination of cycle and lizard.

968.ja: Mimizuzu may be a combination of 蚯蚓 mimizu (earthworm) and 埋める uzumeru (to bury).
968.en: Orthworm may be a combination of ore, ortho- (prefix meaning "straight" or "upright"), orthoclase (a mineral), and earthworm.

969.ja: Kirame may be a combination of キラキラ kirakira (onomatopoeia for glittering) or 煌めく kirameku (to glitter), 芽 me (bud), and possibly killer.
969.en: Glimmet may be a combination of glimmer and the suffix -et (indicating something is small or lesser) or emit.

970.ja: Kiraflor may be a combination of キラキラ kirakira (onomatopoeia for glittering), killer, and flor (Spanish and Portuguese for flower).
970.en: Glimmora may be a combination of glimmer and flora. It may also include ore.

971.ja: Bochi may be derived from 墓地 bochi (graveyard). It may also derive from ポチ Pochi (a common name for a pet dog in Japan) or ひとりぼっち hitoribocchi (loneliness).
971.en: Greavard may be a combination of graveyard, grieve, (a byproduct of a historical method of candlemaking; typically used as dog food), and Briard (a long-haired dog breed).

972.ja: Hakadog may be a combination of 墓 haka (tomb) and dog.
972.en: Houndstone may be a combination of hound and headstone.

973.ja: Karamingo may be a combination of 絡み karami (linkage; entanglement; relationship; involvement) and flamingo.
973.en: Flamigo may be a combination of flamingo and amigo (Spanish and Portuguese for friend).

974.ja: Arukujira may be a combination of 歩く aruku (to walk) and 鯨 kujira (whale).
974.en: Cetoddle may be a combination of or (a sea monster in Greek mythology whose name became the etymology for the word cetacean) and toddle (to walk unsteadily) or toddler.

975.ja: Hulkujira may be a combination of hulk, 歩く aruku (to walk), and 鯨 kujira (whale). It may be a pun on 歯鯨 hakujira (toothed whale).
975.en: Cetitan may be a combination of or (a sea monster in Greek mythology whose name became the etymology for the word cetacean) and titan.

976.ja: Migalusa may be a combination of 身軽 migaru (nimble) and merluza.
976.en: Veluza may be a combination of veloz (Spanish and Portuguese for fast) and merluza (Spanish and Portuguese for hake).

977.ja: Dondozo may be a combination of don (Spanish for lord) and どうぞ dōzo (Japanese for "please (help yourself)"; typically said when offering something). Don also refers to a crime boss, which may allude to Dondozo's relationship with Tatsugiri, who acts as its consigliere. It may also derive from bozo, in reference to Dondozo's dimwitted nature.

978.ja: Tatsugiri may be a combination of 竜 tatsu (Japanese for dragon) and 握り寿司 nigirizushi.

979.ja: Konoyozaru may be a combination of この世を去る kono yo o saru (to leave this world; to die) and 猿 saru (monkey).
979.en: Annihilape may be a combination of annihilate and ape.

980.ja: Dooh may be a combination of 土 do (earth or ground), 毒 doku (poison), 王 ō (king), オオサンショウウオ ōsanshōuo (Japanese giant salamander), and ヌオー Nuoh (Quagsire).
980.en: Clodsire may be a combination of clod (lump of earth), sire or (a genus of salamander), and Quagsire.

981.ja: Rikikirin may be derived from 力 riki (power) and 麒麟 kirin (giraffe) with the name being an inversion of キリンリキ Kirinriki (Girafarig). Again, this may reflect Farigiraf's physical inversion, with its tail moving to its head.
981.en: Farigiraf is an inversion of Girafarig. This may reflect Farigiraf's physical inversion, with its tail moving to its head.

982.ja: Nokokocchi may be derived from a partial repetition of ノコッチ Nokocchi (Dunsparce), for the same reasons mentioned above.
982.en: Dudunsparce may be derived from a partial repetition of Dunsparce and redundant, in reference to the former's multi-segmented appearance as well as having an appearance nearly identical to its pre-Evolution. It may also be a combination of double or dud (possibly referencing its pre-Evolution's almost-useless wings and tendency to remain motionless and inactive, or Dudunsparce's aforementioned rel

983.ja: Dodogezan may be a combination of ど do (very much), 土下座 dogeza (kneeling down on the ground), and 斬 zan (to cut or slay). ドゲザン Dogezan is also the Japanese name for its signature move, Kowtow Cleave.
983.en: Kingambit may be a combination of king and gambit; both words are applicable terms used in chess, including in the opening known as the King's Gambit. It may also be derived from the word karambit.

984.ja: Idainakiba can be taken literally as 偉大な牙 idai na kiba (great tusk).
984.en: Great Tusk is literally great tusk.

985.ja: Sakebushippo can be taken literally as 叫ぶ尻尾 sakebu shippo (screaming tail).
985.en: Scream Tail is literally scream tail.

986.ja: Araburutake can be taken literally as 荒ぶる茸 araburu take (savage mushroom).
986.en: Brute Bonnet is literally brute bonnet. The word bonnet may refer to both and the , a species of mushroom.

987.ja: Habatakukami can be taken literally as 羽ばたく髪 habataku kami (fluttering hair).
987.en: Flutter Mane is literally flutter mane.

988.ja: Chiwohauhane can be taken literally as 地を這う羽 chi o hau hane (wings slithering on the ground).
988.en: Slither Wing is literally slither wing.

989.ja: Sunanokegawa can be taken literally as 砂の毛皮 suna no kegawa (sandy fur).
989.en: Sandy Shocks is literally sandy shocks. The word shock may refer to both a burst of electrical energy and a tuft of hair or fur.

990.ja: Tetsunowadachi can be taken literally as 鉄の轍 tetsu no wadachi (iron treads).
990.en: Iron Treads is literally iron treads.

991.ja: Tetsunotsutsumi can be taken literally as 鉄の包み tetsu no tsutsumi (iron bundle).
991.en: Iron Bundle is literally iron bundle.

992.ja: Tetsunokaina can be taken literally as 鉄の腕 tetsu no kaina (iron arms).
992.en: Iron Hands is literally iron hands.

993.ja: Tetsunokoube can be taken literally as 鉄の首 tetsu no kōbe (iron head).
993.en: Iron Jugulis is literally iron jugulis (with jugulis being derived from the Latin word jugulum, meaning throat or neck).

994.ja: Tetsunodokuga can be taken literally as 鉄の毒蛾 tetsu no dokuga (iron venomous moth). 毒蛾 Dokuga (venomous moth) is also the Japanese name for the , named due to the urticating hairs (which are not actually venomous) found in many tussock moth caterpillars.
994.en: Iron Moth is literally iron moth.

995.ja: Tetsunoibara can be taken literally as 鉄の棘 tetsu no ibara (iron thorn).
995.en: Iron Thorns is literally iron thorns.

996.ja: Sebie may be a combination of 背 se (back) and 冷え hie (coldness).
996.en: Frigibax may be a combination of frigid, back, and axe.

997.ja: Segohru may be a combination of 背 se (back) and 凍る kōru (freeze).
997.en: Arctibax may be a combination of arctic, back, and axe.

998.ja: Seglaive may be a combination of 背 se (back) and glaive (referring to with origins in Europe, or short swords such as the Gladius).
998.en: Baxcalibur may be a combination of back and Excalibur (mythical sword belonging to King Arthur).

999.ja: Collecurei may be a combination of collect, 霊 rei (spirit), and a pun on the phrase これくれ kore kure ("Give me this").
999.en: Gimmighoul may be a combination of gimme (informal pronunciation of "give me"), gimmick, mimic, and ghoul. Altogether, it sounds like the phrase "Give me gold."

1000.ja: Surfugo may be a combination of surf, 富豪 fugō (wealthy person), gold, and possibly go (as in Pokémon GO).
1000.en: Gholdengo may be a combination of ghoul or ghost, golden, and possibly go (as in GO, the game its pre-Evolution Gimmighoul first debuted in; Gholdengo's model itself was in the game's files before it was officially revealed). It may also come from tengo (Spanish for "I have") or деньги den'gi (Russian for money, itself from denga, a historical unit worth 1/200th of a Russian ruble).

1001.ja: 蟲 / 虫（chóng、中国語：虫）、簡 / 简（jiǎn、中国語：竹簡）、木簡 / 木简（中国語：木簡）
1001.zh: Wo-Chien may be a combination of 蝸 / 蜗 wō (Chinese for snail) and 簡 / 简 jiǎn (Chinese for bamboo slips). The name is formatted in Wade–Giles, a romanization system used in the Anglosphere for most of the 20th century.

1002.ja: 豹（bào, 中国語:ヒョウ）、劍 / 剑（jiàn, 中国語:剣）
1002.zh: Chien-Pao and Paojian may be a combination of 劍 / 剑 jiàn (Chinese for sword) or 寶劍 / 宝剑 bǎojiàn (Chinese for treasure sword) and 豹 bào (Chinese for leopard). The English name is formatted in Wade–Giles, a romanization system used in the Anglosphere for most of the 20th century.

1003.ja: 鼎 (Ding, 中国語：鼎）、鹿（lù, 中国語：シカ）
1003.zh: Ting-Lu and Dinlu may be a combination of 鼎 dǐng (ancient Chinese cauldron) and 鹿 lù (Chinese for deer). The English name is formatted in Wade–Giles, a romanization system used in the Anglosphere for most of the 20th century.

1004.ja: 玉（yù, 中国語:玉）、魚 / 鱼（yú, 中国語:魚）
1004.zh: Chi-Yu may be a combination of 鯽魚 / 鲫鱼 jìyú (Chinese for , from which goldfish are domesticated) and 玉 yù (Chinese for jade). The name is formatted in Wade–Giles, a romanization system used in the Anglosphere for most of the 20th century.

1005.ja: Todorokutsuki can be taken literally as 轟く月 todoroku tsuki (roaring moon).
1005.en: Roaring Moon is literally roaring moon.

1006.ja: Tetsunobujin can be taken literally as 鉄の武人 tetsu no bujin (iron warrior).
1006.en: Iron Valiant is literally iron valiant.

1007.ja: Koraidon may be a combination of 古来 korai or 古代 kodai (ancient), ライド raido (ride), and dragon, don (Spanish for lord), or -don (common suffix in dinosaur names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).
1007.en: Its alternate name, Winged King, is literally winged king.

1008.ja: Miraidon may be a combination of 未来 mirai (future), ライド raido (ride), and dragon, don (Spanish for lord), or -don (common suffix in dinosaur names derived from the Ancient Greek word for tooth).
1008.en: Miraidon's name may have been intentionally designed to sound phonetically like me ride on, referencing how the player rides around the Paldea region on it.

1009.ja: Uneruminamo can be taken literally as うねる水面 uneru minamo (undulating water's surface).
1009.en: Walking Wake is literally walking wake. A is a movement of water created by a moving object.

1010.ja: Tetsunoisaha can be taken literally as 鉄の斑葉 tetsu no isaha (iron mottled leaves).
1010.en: Iron Leaves is literally iron leaves.

1011.ja: Kamicchu may be a combination of 果 ka (fruit), 花蜜 kamitsu (nectar), 虫 chū (bug; worm), and カジッチュ Kajicchu (Applin).
1011.en: Dipplin may be a combination of di- (prefix meaning double), dip, and Applin.

1012.ja: Poltchageist may be a combination of poltergeist and 抹茶 matcha, following the same naming pattern as Polteageist.

1013.ja: Sinistcha may be a combination of sinister and 抹茶 matcha, following the same naming pattern as Sinistea.

1014.ja: Iineinu may be a combination of いいね ii ne (interjection to express approval) and 犬 inu (dog).
1014.en: Okidogi may be a combination of okie-dokie (variant of "okay") and dog. "" may also be a reference to training uniforms used for martial arts, hence the Fighting typing.

1015.ja: Mashimashira may be a combination of 増し mashi (better, improving) and 猿 mashira (archaic word for monkey or ape).
1015.en: Munkidori may be a combination of monkey and hunky dory (slang meaning that things are good).

1016.ja: Kichikigisu may be a combination of 吉 kichi (fortune) and 雉子 kigisu (archaic reading of pheasant).
1016.en: Fezandipiti may be a combination of pheasant and serendipity (an unexpected but auspicious happening).

1017.ja: Ogerpon may be derived from ogre and ponkan or dekopon (varieties of orange), as well as ～ぽん -pon, a cutesy suffix applied to names as a form of endearment.

1018.ja: Briduras may be derived from bridge, ジュラルドン Duraludon, and ソーラス -saurus (Greek-derived suffix for dinosaur names).
1018.en: Archaludon may be a combination of arch (meaning principal or superlative, as well as referring to ), Duraludon, and possibly Archosaur.

1019.ja: Kamitsuorochi may be a combination of 果 ka (fruit), 花蜜 kamitsu (nectar), and 八岐大蛇 Yamata no Orochi.
1019.en: Hydrapple may be a combination of , dragon, and apple. The term "syrpent" may be a combination of syrup or wyrm and serpent.

1020.ja: Ugatsuhomura can be taken literally as 穿つ炎 ugatsu homura (piercing flame).
1020.en: Gouging Fire is literally gouging fire.

1021.ja: Takeruraiko can be taken as 猛る雷鼓 takeru raiko (furious thunderclap or furious thunder drum). It may also come from Raikou.
1021.en: Raging Bolt is literally raging bolt.

1022.ja: Tetsunoiwao can be taken literally as 鉄の巌 tetsu no iwao (iron boulder).
1022.en: Iron Boulder is literally iron boulder.

1023.ja: Tetsunokashira can be taken literally as 鉄の頭 tetsu no kashira (iron head; iron leader).
1023.en: Iron Crown is literally iron crown. Crown may refer to the , the top of a person's head, or a reigning monarch or leader.

1024.ja: テラスタル、terrapin（テラピン、北米産の食用亀）、galápagos（スペイン語：ゾウガメたち）
1024.en: Terapagos may be a combination of stal, terrapin, and galápagos (a Spanish word for turtles, most recognizable because of the Galápagos Islands).

1025.ja: Momowarou may be a combination of 桃太郎 Momotarō, 桃 momo (peach), and 悪し waroshi (bad or inferior; the classical Japanese form of 悪い warui) or 和郎 warō (boy).
1025.en: Pecharunt may be a combination of peach or Pecha Berry and runt or petulant.
